partitionByWispStatus) but the fundamental pattern of routing by ID substring
is fragile.

### 6. DOLT-SPECIFIC ASSUMPTIONS THAT BREAK PORTABILITY
- store.go:848 - CALL DOLT_COMMIT during schema init
- versioned.go:52-62 - dolt_diff(), AS OF, DOLT_HASHOF() - Dolt-only functions
- versioned.go:142 - dolt_branches table query
- federation.go:19 - DOLT_PUSH, DOLT_PULL, DOLT_FETCH - Dolt procedures
- versioned.go:253 - DOLT_CONFLICTS_RESOLVE procedure
- store.go:795-796 - CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS with Dolt catalog race workarounds
- history.go:83-86 - dolt_history_issues table (Dolt-specific virtual table)
- versioned.go:204-212 - dolt_log table query

The Storage interface (storage.go) is clean, but the concrete Dolt implementation
uses Dolt-specific stored procedures and virtual tables extensively. If a
non-Dolt backend were added, the versioned/federation features would need
separate interface extension points.

### 7. HARDCODED DEFAULT CONFIG VALUES (schema.go:250-263)
- defaultConfig embeds specific defaults: compact_model = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
  compact_batch_size = 50, types.custom = specific list including "molecule,gate,convoy..."
- These are Gas Town operational constants baked into the storage layer schema init.

### Non-issues (well-designed patterns)
- Custom types/statuses: config.go reads from DB config table with YAML fallback - good
- issue_scan.go: Centralized scan via issueSelectColumns - avoids column drift
- configfile.go: All server params have env var overrides - good for portability
- transactions.go: Clean interface with proper commit semantics
- Test database firewall: Pattern-based guard against test pollution - solid defense","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/obsidian","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T02:42:01Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-02-28T02:49:19Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T02:49:19Z","close_reason":"Completed with no code changes (already fixed or pushed directly to main)"}

{"notes":"Analysis: Tests take 68s. Root cause: 15+ tests each create a separate Dolt database (CREATE DATABASE + schema init = ~0.5-2s each). Fix: refactor tests to use branch-per-test isolation on the shared DB (newTestDoltStore). The path-based check functions (CheckDuplicateIssues, CheckStaleClosedIssues, etc.) internally open stores, so tests calling them create new DBs. Fix by testing the underlying query logic directly with the shared store's sql.DB."}

{"notes":"Analysis complete. Key findings:
- Total wall-clock: 233s for cmd/bd tests
- TestInitDatabaseFlag: 77s (go build subprocess compilation)
- ~95 database creations via newTestStore, each serialized by doltNewMutex
- Most slow tests already use t.Parallel() but doltNewMutex serializes CREATE DATABASE
- Branch-per-test pattern exists in internal/storage/dolt/ but not used in cmd/bd
Strategy:
1. Build bd binary once in TestMain (save ~33s)
2. Migrate newTestStore to shared DB + branch-per-test (save ~50-80s)
3. Expected result: <60s target achievable"}

{"notes":"Findings: Original dolt pkg tests took ~127s (75s baseline from bead). Three optimizations applied: (1) Added t.Parallel() to all branch-isolated tests (setupTestStore calls t.Parallel internally), since each test gets its own Dolt connection+branch. (2) Reduced concurrent stress test iterations - TestReadWriteMix from 5x100 to 3x20, TestHighContentionStress from 20x50 to 8x15, TestSameIssueUpdateRace goroutines from 10 to 5. These test correctness not throughput. (3) Reduced TestDeleteIssuesBatchPerformance from 100 to 25 issues. (4) Capped GOMAXPROCS to 8 in test init() to prevent server overload. Result: 30-35s consistently, down from 127s."}

{"notes":"Implemented: Shared DB with branch-per-test isolation for cmd/bd tests.
Results: ~233s → ~70-90s (60-70% reduction).

1. Shared database (bd_cmd_shared) with committed schema in TestMain
2. newTestStore uses Dolt branches (COW snapshots) instead of CREATE DATABASE per test
3. newIsolatedTestStore for routing/doctor tests needing separate databases
4. buildBDForInitTests uses pre-built binary from ~/.local/bin/bd
5. TestInitDatabaseFlag parallelized"}

- Total wall-clock: 233s for cmd/bd tests
- TestInitDatabaseFlag: 77s (go build subprocess compilation)
- ~95 database creations via newTestStore, each serialized by doltNewMutex

- Branch-per-test pattern exists in internal/storage/dolt/ but not used in cmd/bd

1. Build bd binary once in TestMain (save ~33s)
2. Migrate newTestStore to shared DB + branch-per-test (save ~50-80s)

old_value: {"id":"bd-w2w","title":"bd show can't find wisps: only queries issues table, not wisps table","description":"bd show only queries the issues table but wisps are stored in the wisps table (via wispIssueTable() routing). When refinery or other agents try to bd show a wisp ID, it returns 'not found'. This blocks refinery patrol hook/report operations. Root cause identified by gastown/refinery. Fix: bd show (and related lookup functions like GetIssue) need to check wispIssueTable() routing — if the ID has a wisp pattern, query the wisps table. See beads internal/storage/dolt/wisps.go for the routing logic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T21:50:03Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-02-28T21:50:03Z"}

{"id":"bd-pr-sheriff","title":"PR Sheriff (reference to hq-pr-sheriff)","description":"PR Sheriff standing orders for beads repo (emma). See hq-pr-sheriff for original (lost in DB rebuild). Full workflow doc: gt/docs/pr-sheriff.md

Decision tree: gather open PRs (filter out changes-requested), close drafts, then classify each PR into buckets:
A) Easy-win → merge directly
B) Merge-fix → merge then push follow-up fix
C) Fix-merge → fix locally first, then merge with attribution
D) Cherry-pick-merge → extract valuable fixes from messy PRs, close original
E) Split-merge → separate multi-concern PRs into individual commits
F) Needs attention → farm to crew for deep evaluation + recommendation
G) Superseded → close with explanation

Execute A-E via crew in waves. Farm F to crew for written recommendations. Present F recommendations to overseer as final step.","status":"pinned","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/crew/emma","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:02:09Z","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2026-02-27T07:11:36Z","pinned":true}

Target: under 15s.","notes":"Analysis: Tests take 68s. Root cause: 15+ tests each create a separate Dolt database (CREATE DATABASE + schema init = ~0.5-2s each). Fix: refactor tests to use branch-per-test isolation on the shared DB (newTestDoltStore). The path-based check functions (CheckDuplicateIssues, CheckStaleClosedIssues, etc.) internally open stores, so tests calling them create new DBs. Fix by testing the underlying query logic directly with the shared store's sql.DB.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/onyx","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T03:42:10Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-01T07:10:21Z"}

Target: under 15s.","notes":"Analysis: Tests take 68s. Root cause: 15+ tests each create a separate Dolt database (CREATE DATABASE + schema init = ~0.5-2s each). Fix: refactor tests to use branch-per-test isolation on the shared DB (newTestDoltStore). The path-based check functions (CheckDuplicateIssues, CheckStaleClosedIssues, etc.) internally open stores, so tests calling them create new DBs. Fix by testing the underlying query logic directly with the shared store's sql.DB.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/onyx","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T03:42:10Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-01T07:40:44Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T07:40:44Z","close_reason":"Closed"}

{"id":"bd-6ud","title":"bd doctor: agent-facing diagnostic mode","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-03qm8
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T07:40:04Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Add a mode to bd doctor that outputs diagnostic context designed for agent consumption, not human consumption. Instead of 'FAIL: schema mismatch' it would output 'The beads schema is version 5, expected version 6. The following tables are missing columns: [list]. To fix: run bd migrate. If that fails, the schema migration code is in internal/storage/dolt/migrations.go.' This inverts control: Go observes and reports, the agent decides and acts. ZFC-compliant doctor output.","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"beads/polecats/obsidian","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T07:39:04Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-01T07:40:04Z"}

{"notes":"Review complete. Filed 11 issues:

SECURITY:
- bd-yzlh: SQL injection in clean-databases via backtick-quoted names
- bd-wvxf: Weak encryption key derivation in credentials.go (dbPath-based)
- bd-9k0c: Race window in env var credential passing (federationEnvMutex insufficient)
- bd-6dvy: Shell metachar in suggested error-message commands (low severity)

BUGS:
- bd-msdq: SSH fallback skipped when remoteUser set (Hosted Dolt + SSH breaks)
- bd-zoi2: federation.go PushTo/PullFrom/Fetch missing SSH fallback entirely
- bd-e6o2: Misleading 'SQL + CLI' success message on partial CLI failure
- bd-7dqc: IPv6 parsing bug in testHTTPConnectivity
- bd-99ny: Stale 'mode' in valid keys error message

RELIABILITY:
- bd-x9oj: No timeout on CLI push/pull exec (can hang indefinitely)
- bd-m7ry: Idle monitor PID file orphaned on normal exit paths

{"design":"## Review: Test Infrastructure (internal/testutil + test helpers across packages)

### Files Reviewed
- internal/testutil/testdoltserver.go (300 lines)
- internal/testutil/testdoltserver_windows.go (88 lines)
- internal/testutil/testdoltbranch.go (173 lines)
- internal/testutil/tmpfs.go (62 lines)
- internal/testutil/tmpfs_test.go (63 lines)
- internal/testutil/fixtures/fixtures.go (544 lines)
- internal/testutil/fixtures/fixtures_test.go (129 lines)
- internal/testutil/testdata/dolt-init.sql (8 lines)
- TestMain functions in 14 packages (dolt, migrations, tracker, utils, molecules, config, beads_test, regression, protocol, doctor, doctor/fix, cmd/bd, beads_hash_multiclone, contributor_routing)
- Test helpers: cmd/bd/test_helpers_test.go, cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go, cmd/bd/doctor/test_helpers_test.go

### Issues Filed (5 beads)
1. bd-tn12: Stale SQLite error check in fixtures importFromJSONL (UNIQUE constraint failed vs Duplicate entry)
2. bd-ilcx: Missing timeout in StartIsolatedDoltContainer (context.Background without timeout)
3. bd-sf4e: Dead code: FindFreePort exported but never called
4. bd-aonx: Dead code: xLargeFromJSONL unexported and unused
5. bd-4q6f: DoltDockerImage and utility functions duplicated across build-tagged files

### Areas Reviewed Without Issues
- Testcontainers integration: Well-structured singleton pattern with sync.Once, proper cleanup via container termination
- Branch-per-test isolation: Clean pattern with StartTestBranch/ResetTestBranch/CleanTestBranches, MaxOpenConns=1 requirement documented
- Server crash detection: IsCrashed/CrashError properly handle nil container and reused server cases
- Production port firewall: Port 3307 guard in StartTestDoltServer and SetupSharedTestDB prevents test pollution
- Security: SQL injection properly handled via parameterized queries or nolint:gosec with documented rationale
- Race conditions: TestMain env var management is package-scoped (one binary per package), no cross-package races
- Test pollution: Container-based cleanup removes all databases; shared-server path has known orphan DB risk (handled by gt dolt cleanup)
- Platform skips: Windows build tag properly stubs all container functions with t.Skip
- Error handling: Proper error wrapping throughout, cleanup on failure paths"}

{"design":"# Code Review: Storage Layer & Query Logic (internal/storage/dolt/*.go)

## Scope
26 non-test Go source files in internal/storage/dolt/ (excluding migrations/ subdirectory).

## Files Reviewed
store.go (~1100 LoC), transaction.go (580), queries.go (~1000+), ephemeral_routing.go (282), issues.go (~450), wisps.go (1142), events.go (320), labels.go (221), dependencies.go (1075), config.go (257), errors.go (72), util.go (39), issue_scan.go (199), open.go (141), circuit.go (240), compact.go (165), history.go (271), federation.go (244), rename.go (147), versioned.go (214), noms_lock.go (49), bootstrap.go (167), credentials.go (313), adaptive_length.go (130), metadata_schema.go (132), migrations.go (80)

## Issues Filed (14 beads)

### Security (1)
- bd-rv8g: Encryption key derivation from dbPath is predictable (credentials.go)

### Data Integrity (5)
- bd-2avi: AddDependency/RemoveDependency bypass Dolt versioning (dependencies.go)
- bd-hl48: GetAllEventsSince UNION ALL has auto-increment ID collisions (events.go)
- bd-xe27: Cycle detection does not span wisp_dependencies table (dependencies.go)
- bd-od40: UpdateIssueID does not update wisp table references (rename.go)
- bd-zzji: SET FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS=0 not restored on tx rollback (rename.go)

### Error Handling (3)
- bd-w59l: SearchIssues ephemeral fallthrough silently swallows errors (queries.go)
- bd-57ab: waiters JSON marshal error silently discarded (wisps.go, transaction.go)
- bd-n01d: PromoteFromEphemeral discards event/comment copy errors (ephemeral_routing.go)

### Concurrency (1)
- bd-klgl: getWisp RLock not deferred - panic causes permanent deadlock (wisps.go)

### API Inconsistency (1)
- bd-v6v8: Transaction SearchIssues supports far fewer filters than store-level (transaction.go)

### Maintenance / Code Quality (2)
- bd-is9q: Massive filter-building duplication between searchWisps and SearchIssues (wisps.go, queries.go)
- bd-0jph: addWispLabel/removeWispLabel do not record events (wisps.go)

### Correctness (1)
- bd-8ifp: validateRef rejects valid Dolt branch names with dots or slashes (history.go)

## Themes Observed

1. Wisp/permanent table parity gap: Many operations handle the permanent tables comprehensively but miss the wisp counterparts (rename, cycle detection, event recording, label event audit trail).

2. Inconsistent Dolt versioning: Some write paths use RunInTransaction (which calls DOLT_COMMIT), others use raw tx.Commit() without DOLT_COMMIT. This creates gaps in the version history.

3. Error handling philosophy inconsistency: Some paths use best-effort (_, _ = ...) while others are strict. The fallthrough pattern in SearchIssues silently masks errors under the guise of backwards compatibility.

4. Filter duplication: The two-table architecture (issues + wisps) requires parallel filter-building code that is not shared, creating ~400 lines of duplicated logic that must be kept in sync.

## No Issues Found In
- SQL injection: All user-supplied values use parameterized queries. Dynamic table names are hardcoded constants. Refs are validated with regex before string interpolation.
- Dead SQLite/JSONL references: None found. The codebase is clean of legacy storage references.
- OTel instrumentation: Properly applied to queryContext/execContext.
- Circuit breaker: Well-implemented with file-based cross-process state.
- Batch query patterns: Properly bounded with queryBatchSize to avoid oversized IN-clauses."}

{"design":"## Doctor Subsystem Review — Findings Summary

Reviewed ~14,300 lines across 50 source files in cmd/bd/doctor/ and cmd/bd/doctor/fix/.

### Issues Filed (22 beads)

**High Severity (4):**
- bd-nk9f: Systematic missing rows.Err() checks (~14+ locations)
- bd-a97g: Query errors masked as StatusOK in validation.go
- bd-qa7l: CheckIDFormat always returns 'hash-based' regardless of detection
- bd-k8pu: ParseVersionParts index-out-of-bounds panic in version.go

**Medium Severity (14):**
- bd-l75n: Potential panic on short repo IDs [:8] in CheckRepoFingerprint
- bd-7zej: N+1 query patterns in deep.go and migration_validation.go
- bd-c1g8: Hardcoded root: credentials in perf_dolt.go
- bd-hwmb: os.Exit from library code in perf.go
- bd-lars: Nocgo stubs return StatusOK for skipped checks
- bd-md2p: Relative path usage fragile from subdirectories
- bd-7fsv: Hand-rolled YAML parser in multirepo.go is incorrect
- bd-hwxg: remotes.go Fix field bug
- bd-uz1x: Federation hardcoded port + wrong PID path
- bd-ppnp: fix/database_integrity.go backup restore bug
- bd-s20p: fix/validation.go openDoltDB missing timeout/password
- bd-bbr6: fix/maintenance.go StaleClosedIssues skip logic
- bd-d96s: detectHookMarkerState marker validation
- bd-6gs3: Inconsistent return shape from RunDoltHealthChecksWithLock

**Cleanup/Technical Debt (4):**
- bd-zw81: Stale JSONL/SQLite references across subsystem
- bd-6yqf: Dead code cleanup (7 instances)
- bd-0jkc: Missing test coverage (20+ functions)
- bd-wif2: Hardcoded status strings in legacy.go

**Error Handling (4):**
- bd-mppa: FindOrphanedIssues swallows provider errors
- bd-4hn4: ScanForArtifacts/checkDatabaseConfigValues swallow errors
- bd-0xqi: fix/database_config.go silent no-op for Dolt users
- bd-gzu5: checkDoltLocks masks connection errors

### Key Patterns Observed
1. rows.Err() is systematically missing — only maintenance.go does it correctly
2. Error masking as StatusOK is widespread in validation checks
3. Two potential panics from unguarded slice indexing (version.go, integrity.go)
4. JSONL/SQLite references persist across ~10 files post-Dolt migration
5. Multiple check functions use relative paths instead of accepting root path parameter
6. The fix/ package has divergent DB-open functions (openDoltDB vs openFixDB)"}

cosmetic issues — focus on substantive problems.","design":"# Code Review: Storage Layer & Query Logic (internal/storage/dolt/*.go)

## Scope
26 non-test Go source files in internal/storage/dolt/ (excluding migrations/ subdirectory).

## Files Reviewed
store.go (~1100 LoC), transaction.go (580), queries.go (~1000+), ephemeral_routing.go (282), issues.go (~450), wisps.go (1142), events.go (320), labels.go (221), dependencies.go (1075), config.go (257), errors.go (72), util.go (39), issue_scan.go (199), open.go (141), circuit.go (240), compact.go (165), history.go (271), federation.go (244), rename.go (147), versioned.go (214), noms_lock.go (49), bootstrap.go (167), credentials.go (313), adaptive_length.go (130), metadata_schema.go (132), migrations.go (80)

## Issues Filed (14 beads)

### Security (1)
- bd-rv8g: Encryption key derivation from dbPath is predictable (credentials.go)

### Data Integrity (5)
- bd-2avi: AddDependency/RemoveDependency bypass Dolt versioning (dependencies.go)
- bd-hl48: GetAllEventsSince UNION ALL has auto-increment ID collisions (events.go)
- bd-xe27: Cycle detection does not span wisp_dependencies table (dependencies.go)
- bd-od40: UpdateIssueID does not update wisp table references (rename.go)
- bd-zzji: SET FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS=0 not restored on tx rollback (rename.go)

### Error Handling (3)
- bd-w59l: SearchIssues ephemeral fallthrough silently swallows errors (queries.go)
- bd-57ab: waiters JSON marshal error silently discarded (wisps.go, transaction.go)
- bd-n01d: PromoteFromEphemeral discards event/comment copy errors (ephemeral_routing.go)

### Concurrency (1)
- bd-klgl: getWisp RLock not deferred - panic causes permanent deadlock (wisps.go)

### API Inconsistency (1)
- bd-v6v8: Transaction SearchIssues supports far fewer filters than store-level (transaction.go)

### Maintenance / Code Quality (2)
- bd-is9q: Massive filter-building duplication between searchWisps and SearchIssues (wisps.go, queries.go)
- bd-0jph: addWispLabel/removeWispLabel do not record events (wisps.go)

### Correctness (1)
- bd-8ifp: validateRef rejects valid Dolt branch names with dots or slashes (history.go)

## Themes Observed

1. Wisp/permanent table parity gap: Many operations handle the permanent tables comprehensively but miss the wisp counterparts (rename, cycle detection, event recording, label event audit trail).

2. Inconsistent Dolt versioning: Some write paths use RunInTransaction (which calls DOLT_COMMIT), others use raw tx.Commit() without DOLT_COMMIT. This creates gaps in the version history.

3. Error handling philosophy inconsistency: Some paths use best-effort (_, _ = ...) while others are strict. The fallthrough pattern in SearchIssues silently masks errors under the guise of backwards compatibility.

4. Filter duplication: The two-table architecture (issues + wisps) requires parallel filter-building code that is not shared, creating ~400 lines of duplicated logic that must be kept in sync.

## No Issues Found In
- SQL injection: All user-supplied values use parameterized queries. Dynamic table names are hardcoded constants. Refs are validated with regex before string interpolation.
- Dead SQLite/JSONL references: None found. The codebase is clean of legacy storage references.
- OTel instrumentation: Properly applied to queryContext/execContext.
- Circuit breaker: Well-implemented with file-based cross-process state.
- Batch query patterns: Properly bounded with queryBatchSize to avoid oversized IN-clauses.","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/obsidian","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:03:37Z","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:59Z"}

{"design":"# Review: Features & Integration — Full Findings

## Scope Reviewed
- cmd/bd/create.go (39KB)
- cmd/bd/close.go (12KB)
- cmd/bd/mol*.go (13 files)
- cmd/bd/hooks.go (30KB)
- cmd/bd/dep.go (34KB) + dep_test.go (40KB)
- cmd/bd/list.go (33KB)

## HIGH Severity (5 issues)

1. **bd-ey4f**: createInRig missing Dolt commit for labels AND major feature divergence
   - Labels not committed on cross-rig create (data loss)
   - Missing 10+ features vs main create path (silently ignored)

2. **bd-rev6**: ensureBeadsDirForPath checks for beads.db (stale SQLite artifact)
   - Existence check always fails for Dolt dirs, risking re-init

3. **bd-6py7**: burnWisps deletes without transaction
   - Partial deletion on failure, silently reports success

4. **bd-e1ev**: AdvanceToNextStep TOCTOU race condition
   - Two agents can claim same next step simultaneously

5. **bd-87xv**: watchIssues broken with Dolt
   - Watches for JSONL/.db file changes that never happen with Dolt
   - Signal handler leak and debounce timer race condition

## MEDIUM Severity (9 issues)

6. **bd-31bo**: Hook uninstall gaps (core.hooksPath not reset, misleading --chain, stale help)
7. **bd-gp5b**: Embedded hook templates unused (binary bloat, version drift v0.56.1)
8. **bd-agx1**: close.go swallowed GetIssue error + cross-rig autoClose assumption
9. **bd-jmzc**: dep.go: cannot remove external deps + dead --type flag + no type validation
10. **bd-ttno**: list.go missing input validation (--status, --sort, --type flags)
11. **bd-cg90**: Stale JSONL/SQLite references across codebase (5+ locations)
12. **bd-pr9v**: mol_stale.go --json flag shadows persistent root flag
13. **bd-kzp3**: findGateReadyMolecules hardcoded limits silently truncate
14. **bd-hz8h**: Missing test coverage for critical paths
15. **bd-hn4q**: findParentMolecule N+1 queries

## NOT Filed (LOW / cosmetic)
- Dead isRebaseInProgress() function (hooks.go)
- Wasteful strings.Builder in getHookVersion
- getPinnedMolecule shells out on every commit
- No msgFile path validation (defense-in-depth)
- Asymmetric blank line handling in inject/remove section
- close.go escalated flag produces identical behavior
- close.go SetLastTouchedID not called after close
- sanitizeFormulaName compiles regexes per call
- idToStepID can produce duplicate step IDs
- getVarNames non-deterministic order
- mol_ready_gated_test.go stale test setup
- TODOs in mol_squash.go and mol_bond.go
- minPriority semantics (0 vs unset)
- withStorage/dbPath dead code in list.go
- dep direction validation inconsistency
- rootBlocked ignores StatusBlocked children
- warnIfCyclesExist redundant after AddDependency"}

4. **bd-e1ev**: AdvanceToNextStep TOCTOU race condition
   - Two agents can claim same next step simultaneously

5. **bd-87xv**: watchIssues broken with Dolt
   - Watches for JSONL/.db file changes that never happen with Dolt
   - Signal handler leak and debounce timer race condition

old_value: {"id":"bd-wvxf","title":"credentials.go: encryption key derived from dbPath is weak and portable","description":"In credentials.go encryptionKey() (line 47), the AES-256 key is derived from SHA-256(dbPath + 'beads-federation-key-v1'). This means: (1) anyone who knows the dbPath can derive the key and decrypt stored passwords — the path is not a secret, (2) if the database directory is moved/renamed, all stored federation credentials become unreadable, (3) the static salt 'beads-federation-key-v1' provides no real entropy. The comment acknowledges this ('For production, consider using system keyring or external secret managers') but this should be tracked. Stored credentials in the federation_peers table are effectively obfuscated, not encrypted.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:06:53Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:06:53Z"}

Fix: Replace 'os.Getenv("GT_ROOT") == ""' with '!doltserver.IsDaemonManaged()' to match main.go."}

In doltutil/remotes.go:46, AddCLIRemote passes these as exec.Command arguments which is safe (Go's exec.Command does not invoke a shell). However, the Warning message on line 647 suggests running: 'cd %s \u0026\u0026 dolt remote add %s %s' which interpolates the name and url into a shell command suggestion. If a user copies that command, and name/url contain shell metacharacters, this could be exploited. The same pattern exists in RemoveCLIRemote's error message (dolt.go:821). Low severity since it's just a suggested command, but worth noting.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:06:18Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:06:18Z"}

If hooks were installed with --beads or --shared, core.hooksPath still points to .beads/hooks/ after uninstall, silently breaking all hooks. (2) --chain flag (lines 317-319) produces misleading success message claiming existing hooks renamed to .old -- this is a lie since section markers handle it differently. {"description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-zmnf4
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work

dispatched_by: mayor

MEDIUM: hooks.go has three related issues: (1) uninstallHooks() (lines 542-598) never resets core.hooksPath after uninstall. If hooks were installed with --beads or --shared, core.hooksPath still points to .beads/hooks/ after uninstall, silently breaking all hooks. (2) --chain flag (lines 317-319) produces misleading success message claiming existing hooks renamed to .old -- this is a lie since section markers handle it differently. (3) Hook help text (lines 287-294) claims pre-commit syncs database, post-merge syncs, etc. -- none of this actually happens. old_value: {"id":"bd-rv8g","title":"Encryption key derivation from dbPath is predictable","description":"credentials.go:47-53: encryptionKey() derives AES-256 key from SHA256(dbPath + hardcoded-salt). The dbPath is predictable (e.g., ~/.beads/.dolt), making the key guessable. Anyone with knowledge of the file path can decrypt stored federation passwords. The salt is a hardcoded string, not a random value. Additionally, moving the database to a different path silently breaks all encrypted credentials. Suggested fix: use a randomly-generated key stored securely (e.g., in a separate file with restrictive permissions, or system keyring), or at minimum add a per-installation random salt.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:01Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:01Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-e1ev","title":"AdvanceToNextStep TOCTOU race condition in multi-agent environment","description":"HIGH: mol_current.go AdvanceToNextStep() (lines 514-577) reads molecule progress, identifies next ready step, then writes to it -- all without a transaction. In multi-agent Gas Town, two agents could simultaneously close different steps, both identify the same next ready step, and both try to claim it. Classic read-then-write TOCTOU race. Fix: wrap read-identify-update in transact() or add optimistic concurrency control.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:37Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/topaz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:37Z"}

migrate_safety.go:121 verifyServerTarget() opens a MySQL connection with hardcoded DSN 'root@tcp(%s)/' — no password, always user 'root'. However, the Dolt server may be configured with different credentials (via metadata.json or env vars). If the server requires authentication, the verification query will fail even when the actual migration would succeed with the correct credentials.

By contrast, verifyMigrationData() (line 205) correctly accepts user/password parameters from the caller.

Fix: Add user and password parameters to verifyServerTarget() and use them in the DSN. Update callers in runMigrationPhases() to pass the configured credentials."}

old_value: {"id":"bd-0jph","title":"addWispLabel/removeWispLabel don't record events","description":"wisps.go:1121-1141: addWispLabel and removeWispLabel perform INSERT/DELETE on wisp_labels but do NOT record events in wisp_events. In contrast, the permanent AddLabel/RemoveLabel (labels.go) DO record events via execContext INSERT INTO events. This means wisp label changes have no audit trail. Users running 'bd log' on a wisp won't see label changes. Suggested fix: add recordEventInTable calls for EventLabelAdded/EventLabelRemoved, matching labels.go behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:33Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:33Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-wn3n","title":"withPeerCredentials: continues without mutex when peer has no credentials","description":"In credentials.go withPeerCredentials (line 283), when a peer has no credentials (peer.Username and peer.Password are both empty), the code skips the mutex and runs fn() without any locking. But if another goroutine concurrently runs withPeerCredentials WITH credentials, the env vars could be set while this no-credential fn() is executing. The no-credential path's dolt CLI operations (if any) would inherit stale or concurrent credential env vars. The mutex should protect all federation operations, not just ones with explicit credentials.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:33Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:33Z"}

new_value: {"assignee":"beads/polecats/jasper","status":"hooked"}
old_value: {"id":"bd-2avi","title":"AddDependency/RemoveDependency bypass Dolt versioning","description":"dependencies.go:16-147: AddDependency and RemoveDependency use raw s.db.BeginTx + tx.Commit() but do NOT call DOLT_COMMIT afterwards. Compare with RunInTransaction (transaction.go:46-89) which explicitly calls DOLT_COMMIT('-Am', ...) after sqlTx.Commit(). This means dependency changes persist in the working set but are NOT recorded in Dolt's version history (dolt log, time-travel queries). They only become versioned when a later operation happens to call DOLT_COMMIT. Suggested fix: either call DOLT_COMMIT after tx.Commit() in these methods, or refactor to use RunInTransaction.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:05Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:05Z"}

In credentials.go withPeerCredentials (line 283), when a peer has no credentials (peer.Username and peer.Password are both empty), the code skips the mutex and runs fn() without any locking. But if another goroutine concurrently runs withPeerCredentials WITH credentials, the env vars could be set while this no-credential fn() is executing. The no-credential path's dolt CLI operations (if any) would inherit stale or concurrent credential env vars. The mutex should protect all federation operations, not just ones with explicit credentials."}

old_value: {"id":"bd-9k0c","title":"setFederationCredentials: race window between env set and SQL exec","description":"In credentials.go setFederationCredentials (line 264), DOLT_REMOTE_USER and DOLT_REMOTE_PASSWORD are set as process-wide environment variables. The federationEnvMutex serializes federation operations, but it does NOT protect against non-federation code that might also read these env vars or against other goroutines that might exec dolt CLI commands. In store.go Push/Pull, the SQL CALL is made between Setenv and Unsetenv — if another goroutine runs a dolt CLI command (like doltCLIPush) during that window, it would inherit the federation credentials. The env-var approach is fundamentally fragile for concurrent use.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:02Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:02Z"}

In credentials.go setFederationCredentials (line 264), DOLT_REMOTE_USER and DOLT_REMOTE_PASSWORD are set as process-wide environment variables. The federationEnvMutex serializes federation operations, but it does NOT protect against non-federation code that might also read these env vars or against other goroutines that might exec dolt CLI commands. In store.go Push/Pull, the SQL CALL is made between Setenv and Unsetenv — if another goroutine runs a dolt CLI command (like doltCLIPush) during that window, it would inherit the federation credentials. The env-var approach is fundamentally fragile for concurrent use."}

In credentials.go setFederationCredentials (line 264), DOLT_REMOTE_USER and DOLT_REMOTE_PASSWORD are set as process-wide environment variables. The federationEnvMutex serializes federation operations, but it does NOT protect against non-federation code that might also read these env vars or against other goroutines that might exec dolt CLI commands. In store.go Push/Pull, the SQL CALL is made between Setenv and Unsetenv — if another goroutine runs a dolt CLI command (like doltCLIPush) during that window, it would inherit the federation credentials. The env-var approach is fundamentally fragile for concurrent use.","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/topaz","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:02Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:23:52Z"}

new_value: {"assignee":"beads/polecats/topaz","status":"hooked"}
old_value: {"id":"bd-zzji","title":"SET FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS=0 not restored on tx rollback","description":"rename.go:23-106: UpdateIssueID does SET FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS = 0 then re-enables with SET FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS = 1 before tx.Commit(). However, SET statements are session-level in MySQL — they take effect immediately and are NOT rolled back by a transaction rollback. If any operation between disable (line 23) and re-enable (line 103) fails, the defer tx.Rollback() fires but FK checks remain disabled on that pooled connection. Subsequent operations using that connection from the pool will run without FK constraint enforcement. Suggested fix: use a defer to ensure FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS is re-enabled regardless of error path, e.g., defer tx.ExecContext(ctx, 'SET FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS = 1').","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:05Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:05Z"}

{"id":"bd-zw81","title":"Stale JSONL/SQLite references across doctor subsystem","description":"Multiple files contain stale references to JSONL and SQLite that should be updated post-Dolt migration:

1. git.go line ~576: FixMergeDriver sets merge driver name to 'Beads JSONL merge driver'
2. validation.go line ~351: CheckGitConflicts only scans *.jsonl files, ignoring other text files
3. installation.go lines ~93-163: CheckUntrackedBeadsFiles entire check looks for untracked .jsonl files
4. agent.go lines ~718-722: enrichUntrackedFiles references JSONL files
5. gitignore.go lines ~374-387: CheckRedirectTargetValid falls through to checking for beads.db (SQLite)
6. federation.go lines 54,146,263,356,447: return 'N/A (SQLite backend)' messages
7. legacy.go line ~220: CheckDatabaseConfig checks for .db files (SQLite-only)
8. maintenance.go line ~390: message references 'JSONL fallback'
9. role.go line ~31: comment says 'stored in SQLite'
10. legacy.go line ~360: countJSONLIssuesAndPrefix uses default scanner buffer (64KB) unlike migration_validation.go which uses 2MB

Suggested fix: Remove or update references. For checks that only apply to legacy backends, either remove them or clearly gate and document them as legacy-only.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:09Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:09Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-e6o2","title":"dolt remote add: partial failure leaves SQL/CLI surfaces out of sync","description":"In cmd/bd/dolt.go doltRemoteAddCmd (lines 630-649), the remote is first added to the SQL server, then to CLI. If the SQL add succeeds but CLI fails, the code prints a warning but reports success ('Added remote ... (SQL + CLI)' on line 658). The user sees a success message with the '(SQL + CLI)' suffix even though CLI failed. The success message should accurately reflect which surfaces succeeded. Similarly in doltRemoteRemoveCmd (lines 807-838), if SQL removal succeeds but CLI removal fails, the success message says 'Removed remote ... (SQL + CLI)' which is misleading.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:10Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:10Z"}

{"description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-k0qo3
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:24:31Z
dispatched_by: mayor

In cmd/bd/dolt.go doltRemoteAddCmd (lines 630-649), the remote is first added to the SQL server, then to CLI. If the SQL add succeeds but CLI fails, the code prints a warning but reports success ('Added remote ... (SQL + CLI)' on line 658). The user sees a success message with the '(SQL + CLI)' suffix even though CLI failed. The success message should accurately reflect which surfaces succeeded. Similarly in doltRemoteRemoveCmd (lines 807-838), if SQL removal succeeds but CLI removal fails, the success message says 'Removed remote ... (SQL + CLI)' which is misleading."}

old_value: {"id":"bd-klgl","title":"getWisp RLock not deferred - panic causes permanent deadlock","description":"wisps.go:325-343: getWisp acquires s.mu.RLock() at line 327 but does not use defer to release it. Instead, it manually calls RUnlock at three separate return points (lines 329, 334, 341). If scanIssueFromTable panics (e.g., nil pointer, index out of range), the RLock is never released, permanently deadlocking any writer trying to acquire the write lock. Suggested fix: restructure to use defer s.mu.RUnlock() or use a helper closure that defers the unlock.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:16Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:16Z"}

wisps.go:325-343: getWisp acquires s.mu.RLock() at line 327 but does not use defer to release it. Instead, it manually calls RUnlock at three separate return points (lines 329, 334, 341). If scanIssueFromTable panics (e.g., nil pointer, index out of range), the RLock is never released, permanently deadlocking any writer trying to acquire the write lock. Suggested fix: restructure to use defer s.mu.RUnlock() or use a helper closure that defers the unlock."}

Instead, it manually calls RUnlock at three separate return points (lines 329, 334, 341). If scanIssueFromTable panics (e.g., nil pointer, index out of range), the RLock is never released, permanently deadlocking any writer trying to acquire the write lock. Suggested fix: restructure to use defer s.mu.RUnlock() or use a helper closure that defers the unlock.","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/amber","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:16Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:24:46Z"}

{"id":"bd-3hfp","title":"Gas Town detection inconsistency in migrate_dolt.go","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-mpgpw
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:22:53Z
dispatched_by: mayor

migrate_dolt.go:106 uses raw env var check 'os.Getenv("GT_ROOT") == ""' to detect Gas Town, while main.go:541 uses the centralized doltserver.IsDaemonManaged() function. If the detection logic changes (e.g., filesystem heuristic fallback), migrate_dolt.go won't pick up the change.

Location: migrate_dolt.go line 106 (AutoStart computation in handleToDoltMigration)

Fix: Replace 'os.Getenv("GT_ROOT") == ""' with '!doltserver.IsDaemonManaged()' to match main.go.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/obsidian","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:09:07Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/opal","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:24:47Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T18:24:47Z","close_reason":"Closed"}

new_value: {"description":"In cmd/bd/dolt.go doltRemoteAddCmd (line 646), the CLI remote add path calls doltutil.AddCLIRemote(dbPath, name, url) where name and url come directly from user args (args[0], args[1]). In doltutil/remotes.go:46, AddCLIRemote passes these as exec.Command arguments which is safe (Go's exec.Command does not invoke a shell). However, the Warning message on line 647 suggests running: 'cd %s \u0026\u0026 dolt remote add %s %s' which interpolates the name and url into a shell command suggestion. If a user copies that command, and name/url contain shell metacharacters, this could be exploited. The same pattern exists in RemoveCLIRemote's error message (dolt.go:821). Low severity since it's just a suggested command, but worth noting."}

In cmd/bd/dolt.go doltRemoteAddCmd (line 646), the CLI remote add path calls doltutil.AddCLIRemote(dbPath, name, url) where name and url come directly from user args (args[0], args[1]). In doltutil/remotes.go:46, AddCLIRemote passes these as exec.Command arguments which is safe (Go's exec.Command does not invoke a shell). However, the Warning message on line 647 suggests running: 'cd %s && dolt remote add %s %s' which interpolates the name and url into a shell command suggestion. If a user copies that command, and name/url contain shell metacharacters, this could be exploited. The same pattern exists in RemoveCLIRemote's error message (dolt.go:821). Low severity since it's just a suggested command, but worth noting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/quartz","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:06:18Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:25:10Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T18:25:10Z","close_reason":"Closed"}

{"id":"bd-e1ev","title":"AdvanceToNextStep TOCTOU race condition in multi-agent environment","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-lmi9f
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:23:13Z
dispatched_by: mayor

HIGH: mol_current.go AdvanceToNextStep() (lines 514-577) reads molecule progress, identifies next ready step, then writes to it -- all without a transaction. In multi-agent Gas Town, two agents could simultaneously close different steps, both identify the same next ready step, and both try to claim it. Classic read-then-write TOCTOU race. Fix: wrap read-identify-update in transact() or add optimistic concurrency control.","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/onyx","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:37Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/topaz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:23:14Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-9k0c","title":"setFederationCredentials: race window between env set and SQL exec","description":"In credentials.go setFederationCredentials (line 264), DOLT_REMOTE_USER and DOLT_REMOTE_PASSWORD are set as process-wide environment variables. The federationEnvMutex serializes federation operations, but it does NOT protect against non-federation code that might also read these env vars or against other goroutines that might exec dolt CLI commands. In store.go Push/Pull, the SQL CALL is made between Setenv and Unsetenv — if another goroutine runs a dolt CLI command (like doltCLIPush) during that window, it would inherit the federation credentials. The env-var approach is fundamentally fragile for concurrent use.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:02Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:23:53Z"}

new_value: {"description":"In credentials.go withPeerCredentials (line 283), when a peer has no credentials (peer.Username and peer.Password are both empty), the code skips the mutex and runs fn() without any locking. But if another goroutine concurrently runs withPeerCredentials WITH credentials, the env vars could be set while this no-credential fn() is executing. The no-credential path's dolt CLI operations (if any) would inherit stale or concurrent credential env vars. The mutex should protect all federation operations, not just ones with explicit credentials."}

old_value: {"id":"bd-4q6f","title":"DoltDockerImage and utility functions duplicated across build-tagged files","description":"DoltDockerImage constant, FindFreePort(), and WaitForServer() are identically defined in both testdoltserver.go (non-windows) and testdoltserver_windows.go (windows). These are platform-independent: DoltDockerImage is a string constant, FindFreePort is pure TCP, WaitForServer is pure TCP. Duplication means version pin changes or bug fixes must be applied to both files, risking drift. Fix: extract these into a shared file without build tags (e.g., testdoltcommon.go), keeping only the platform-divergent container logic in the build-tagged files.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:07Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/onyx","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:07Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-7dqc","title":"testHTTPConnectivity: incorrect parsing when host contains colon (IPv6)","description":"In cmd/bd/dolt.go testHTTPConnectivity (line 1241), the code checks if host 'Contains \":\"' to detect if a port is already present. But IPv6 addresses also contain colons (e.g., https://[::1]:8080/path). After stripping the scheme and path, an IPv6 address like '[::1]:8080' would match the Contains(':') check, setting port to '' and using the raw host as addr. The resulting dial would fail or connect to the wrong endpoint. Should use net.SplitHostPort or check for bracket-enclosed IPv6 first.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:40Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:40Z"}

{"description":"rename.go:23-106: UpdateIssueID does SET FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS = 0 then re-enables with SET FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS = 1 before tx.Commit(). However, SET statements are session-level in MySQL — they take effect immediately and are NOT rolled back by a transaction rollback. If any operation between disable (line 23) and re-enable (line 103) fails, the defer tx.Rollback() fires but FK checks remain disabled on that pooled connection. Subsequent operations using that connection from the pool will run without FK constraint enforcement. Suggested fix: use a defer to ensure FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS is re-enabled regardless of error path, e.g., defer tx.ExecContext(ctx, 'SET FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS = 1')."}

old_value: {"id":"bd-e6o2","title":"dolt remote add: partial failure leaves SQL/CLI surfaces out of sync","description":"In cmd/bd/dolt.go doltRemoteAddCmd (lines 630-649), the remote is first added to the SQL server, then to CLI. If the SQL add succeeds but CLI fails, the code prints a warning but reports success ('Added remote ... (SQL + CLI)' on line 658). The user sees a success message with the '(SQL + CLI)' suffix even though CLI failed. The success message should accurately reflect which surfaces succeeded. Similarly in doltRemoteRemoveCmd (lines 807-838), if SQL removal succeeds but CLI removal fails, the success message says 'Removed remote ... (SQL + CLI)' which is misleading.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:10Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:24:33Z"}

dispatched_by: mayor

In cmd/bd/dolt.go doltRemoteAddCmd (lines 630-649), the remote is first added to the SQL server, then to CLI. If the SQL add succeeds but CLI fails, the code prints a warning but reports success ('Added remote ... (SQL + CLI)' on line 658). The user sees a success message with the '(SQL + CLI)' suffix even though CLI failed. The success message should accurately reflect which surfaces succeeded. Similarly in doltRemoteRemoveCmd (lines 807-838), if SQL removal succeeds but CLI removal fails, the success message says 'Removed remote ... (SQL + CLI)' which is misleading."}

old_value: {"id":"bd-n01d","title":"PromoteFromEphemeral discards event/comment copy errors","description":"ephemeral_routing.go:251-262: PromoteFromEphemeral copies events and comments from wisp tables to permanent tables using '_, _ = s.execContext(...)' — both result and error are discarded. Then line 265 calls deleteWisp which removes the wisp and all wisp_* auxiliary records. If the copy fails (e.g., connection error, constraint violation), the promotion completes successfully but events/comments are permanently lost. Suggested fix: at minimum log the error; ideally make event/comment copy a required step or warn the user if data was lost during promotion.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:58Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:58Z"}

{"description":"wisps.go:325-343: getWisp acquires s.mu.RLock() at line 327 but does not use defer to release it. Instead, it manually calls RUnlock at three separate return points (lines 329, 334, 341). If scanIssueFromTable panics (e.g., nil pointer, index out of range), the RLock is never released, permanently deadlocking any writer trying to acquire the write lock. Suggested fix: restructure to use defer s.mu.RUnlock() or use a helper closure that defers the unlock."}

A timeout on the wisps query would result in silently returning stale/wrong results from the issues table. Suggested fix: distinguish between 'table does not exist' errors (legitimate fallthrough) and real errors (should be returned). Same pattern at lines 29-34 for allEphemeral(filter.IDs).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:09Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:09Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-rv8g","title":"Encryption key derivation from dbPath is predictable","description":"credentials.go:47-53: encryptionKey() derives AES-256 key from SHA256(dbPath + hardcoded-salt). The dbPath is predictable (e.g., ~/.beads/.dolt), making the key guessable. Anyone with knowledge of the file path can decrypt stored federation passwords. The salt is a hardcoded string, not a random value. Additionally, moving the database to a different path silently breaks all encrypted credentials. Suggested fix: use a randomly-generated key stored securely (e.g., in a separate file with restrictive permissions, or system keyring), or at minimum add a per-installation random salt.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:01Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:23:08Z"}

Suggested fix: use a randomly-generated key stored securely (e.g., in a separate file with restrictive permissions, or system keyring), or at minimum add a per-installation random salt.","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/amber","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:01Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:28:04Z"}

new_value: {"description":"wisps.go:384-385 and transaction.go:284-285: json.Marshal(value) for the waiters field discards the error with '_'. If marshaling fails (e.g., value contains an unsupported type like a channel), an empty or invalid JSON string is written to the database. This could corrupt the waiters field silently. Suggested fix: return the error: 'waitersJSON, err := json.Marshal(value); if err \\!= nil { return fmt.Errorf(\"invalid waiters: %w\", err) }'"}

{"description":"dependencies.go:16-147: AddDependency and RemoveDependency use raw s.db.BeginTx + tx.Commit() but do NOT call DOLT_COMMIT afterwards. Compare with RunInTransaction (transaction.go:46-89) which explicitly calls DOLT_COMMIT('-Am', ...) after sqlTx.Commit(). This means dependency changes persist in the working set but are NOT recorded in Dolt's version history (dolt log, time-travel queries). They only become versioned when a later operation happens to call DOLT_COMMIT. Suggested fix: either call DOLT_COMMIT after tx.Commit() in these methods, or refactor to use RunInTransaction."}

new_value: {"description":"In internal/testutil/fixtures/fixtures.go:467, importFromJSONL checks for 'UNIQUE constraint failed' which is a SQLite error message. Dolt (MySQL-compatible) reports duplicate key violations as 'Duplicate entry' (MySQL error 1062). This check never matches on Dolt, so duplicate-ID errors propagate as failures instead of being silently skipped. Fix: change the error string match to also check for 'Duplicate entry' or use MySQL error code 1062."}

old_value: {"id":"bd-8ifp","title":"validateRef rejects valid Dolt branch names with dots or slashes","description":"history.go:15: validRefPattern = ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ rejects branch names containing dots or slashes. Dolt and git branch names commonly contain dots (e.g., 'release/v2.0', 'feature/auth.flow'). This pattern is used by getIssueAsOf, Diff, and CommitExists. Callers using branch names with dots or slashes will get 'invalid ref format' errors. Suggested fix: expand the pattern to allow dots and forward slashes: ^[a-zA-Z0-9_./-]+$ or use a more permissive pattern that excludes only dangerous characters (quotes, semicolons, backticks).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:10Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:10Z"}

{"id":"bd-ey4f","title":"createInRig missing Dolt commit for labels and major feature divergence","description":"HIGH: create.go createInRig() (lines 834-993) has two critical issues:

1. Missing Dolt commit for labels (lines 974-979): Labels added during cross-rig creation
   are not committed to Dolt. The main create path (lines 743-748) explicitly calls
   store.Commit() for post-create metadata, but createInRig never commits. Labels sit
   in the Dolt working set and can be lost on push/sync/restart. Same class of bug as GH#2009.

2. Major feature divergence: createInRig is missing compared to the main create path:
   - --deps flag (dependency creation)
   - --parent flag (parent-child relationships, label inheritance)
   - --estimate, --validate flags
   - --waits-for / --waits-for-gate flags
   - --no-inherit-labels handling
   - Agent label auto-add logic
   - Hook execution (hookRunner.Run)
   - SetLastTouchedID tracking
   - discoveredFromParentID / SourceRepo inheritance
   - MolType/WispType validation (no IsValid() check)
   - Event flag validation (event metadata on non-event types)

   Users creating cross-rig issues with any of these flags silently have them ignored.

Fix: Consolidate into a single code path operating on targetStore, or add all missing
features to createInRig. Consolidation strongly preferred to prevent ongoing divergence.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:15Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/topaz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:15Z"}

{"id":"bd-ey4f","title":"createInRig missing Dolt commit for labels and major feature divergence","description":"HIGH: create.go createInRig() (lines 834-993) has two critical issues:

1. Missing Dolt commit for labels (lines 974-979): Labels added during cross-rig creation
   are not committed to Dolt. The main create path (lines 743-748) explicitly calls
   store.Commit() for post-create metadata, but createInRig never commits. Labels sit
   in the Dolt working set and can be lost on push/sync/restart. Same class of bug as GH#2009.

2. Major feature divergence: createInRig is missing compared to the main create path:
   - --deps flag (dependency creation)
   - --parent flag (parent-child relationships, label inheritance)
   - --estimate, --validate flags
   - --waits-for / --waits-for-gate flags
   - --no-inherit-labels handling
   - Agent label auto-add logic
   - Hook execution (hookRunner.Run)
   - SetLastTouchedID tracking
   - discoveredFromParentID / SourceRepo inheritance
   - MolType/WispType validation (no IsValid() check)
   - Event flag validation (event metadata on non-event types)

   Users creating cross-rig issues with any of these flags silently have them ignored.

Fix: Consolidate into a single code path operating on targetStore, or add all missing
features to createInRig. Consolidation strongly preferred to prevent ongoing divergence.","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/flint","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:15Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/topaz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:28:50Z"}

{"id":"bd-8ifp","title":"validateRef rejects valid Dolt branch names with dots or slashes","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-l1exc
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:28:43Z
dispatched_by: mayor

history.go:15: validRefPattern = ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ rejects branch names containing dots or slashes. Dolt and git branch names commonly contain dots (e.g., 'release/v2.0', 'feature/auth.flow'). This pattern is used by getIssueAsOf, Diff, and CommitExists. Callers using branch names with dots or slashes will get 'invalid ref format' errors. Suggested fix: expand the pattern to allow dots and forward slashes: ^[a-zA-Z0-9_./-]+$ or use a more permissive pattern that excludes only dangerous characters (quotes, semicolons, backticks).","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/flint","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:10Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:28:44Z"}

new_value: {"description":"dependencies.go:16-147: AddDependency and RemoveDependency use raw s.db.BeginTx + tx.Commit() but do NOT call DOLT_COMMIT afterwards. Compare with RunInTransaction (transaction.go:46-89) which explicitly calls DOLT_COMMIT('-Am', ...) after sqlTx.Commit(). This means dependency changes persist in the working set but are NOT recorded in Dolt's version history (dolt log, time-travel queries). They only become versioned when a later operation happens to call DOLT_COMMIT. Suggested fix: either call DOLT_COMMIT after tx.Commit() in these methods, or refactor to use RunInTransaction."}

old_value: {"id":"bd-m7ry","title":"dolt idle-monitor: PID file not cleaned up on all exit paths","description":"In cmd/bd/dolt.go doltIdleMonitorCmd (line 363), the PID file is written immediately but only cleaned up in the SIGTERM/SIGINT handler (line 383). If the monitor exits normally (e.g., idle timeout disabled at line 371 with early return, or RunIdleMonitor returns normally at line 387), the PID file is left behind as an orphan. The early return at line 371 when idle timeout is disabled ('0') exits without cleaning up the PID file it just wrote on line 363. Should defer PID file cleanup.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:25Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:25Z"}

In cmd/bd/dolt.go doltIdleMonitorCmd (line 363), the PID file is written immediately but only cleaned up in the SIGTERM/SIGINT handler (line 383). If the monitor exits normally (e.g., idle timeout disabled at line 371 with early return, or RunIdleMonitor returns normally at line 387), the PID file is left behind as an orphan. The early return at line 371 when idle timeout is disabled ('0') exits without cleaning up the PID file it just wrote on line 363. Should defer PID file cleanup."}

new_value: {"assignee":"beads/polecats/malachite","status":"hooked"}
old_value: {"id":"bd-hl48","title":"GetAllEventsSince UNION ALL has auto-increment ID collisions","description":"events.go:61-78: GetAllEventsSince uses UNION ALL between events and wisp_events tables, both of which use independent auto-increment IDs. The result is ORDER BY id ASC, but both tables can have events with the same ID (e.g., both tables have row ID=5). This produces ambiguous ordering and can cause event-streaming consumers to miss events or process them out of order. Suggested fix: either add a table discriminator to the ORDER BY (e.g., ORDER BY table_source, id ASC), use UNION ALL with created_at ordering instead, or use a single shared sequence.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:38Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:38Z"}

new_value: {"description":"HIGH: mol_current.go AdvanceToNextStep() (lines 514-577) reads molecule progress, identifies next ready step, then writes to it -- all without a transaction. In multi-agent Gas Town, two agents could simultaneously close different steps, both identify the same next ready step, and both try to claim it. Classic read-then-write TOCTOU race. Fix: wrap read-identify-update in transact() or add optimistic concurrency control."}

If a wisp has a dependency pointing to a renamed issue (e.g., wisp_dependencies.depends_on_id = oldID), that reference becomes dangling after the rename. new_value: {"description":"DoltDockerImage constant, FindFreePort(), and WaitForServer() are identically defined in both testdoltserver.go (non-windows) and testdoltserver_windows.go (windows). These are platform-independent: DoltDockerImage is a string constant, FindFreePort is pure TCP, WaitForServer is pure TCP. Duplication means version pin changes or bug fixes must be applied to both files, risking drift. Fix: extract these into a shared file without build tags (e.g., testdoltcommon.go), keeping only the platform-divergent container logic in the build-tagged files."}

Cross-table cycles involving wisp_dependencies are not detected. For example: permanent issue A depends on wisp B (in wisp_dependencies), wisp B depends on permanent issue A (in dependencies) — this cycle is not caught. Similarly, DetectCycles (line 694) only queries GetAllDependencyRecords which only reads the permanent dependencies table. Suggested fix: extend the cycle detection CTE to UNION both dependency tables, or run the check against both tables.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:42Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:42Z"}

{"description":"In cmd/bd/dolt.go doltRemoteAddCmd (lines 630-649), the remote is first added to the SQL server, then to CLI. If the SQL add succeeds but CLI fails, the code prints a warning but reports success ('Added remote ... (SQL + CLI)' on line 658). The user sees a success message with the '(SQL + CLI)' suffix even though CLI failed. The success message should accurately reflect which surfaces succeeded. Similarly in doltRemoteRemoveCmd (lines 807-838), if SQL removal succeeds but CLI removal fails, the success message says 'Removed remote ... (SQL + CLI)' which is misleading."}

new_value: {"description":"In cmd/bd/dolt.go testHTTPConnectivity (line 1241), the code checks if host 'Contains \":\"' to detect if a port is already present. But IPv6 addresses also contain colons (e.g., https://[::1]:8080/path). After stripping the scheme and path, an IPv6 address like '[::1]:8080' would match the Contains(':') check, setting port to '' and using the raw host as addr. The resulting dial would fail or connect to the wrong endpoint. Should use net.SplitHostPort or check for bracket-enclosed IPv6 first."}
old_value:
{"id":"bd-7dqc","title":"testHTTPConnectivity: incorrect parsing when host contains colon (IPv6)","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-ycm1j
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:26:41Z
dispatched_by: mayor

In cmd/bd/dolt.go testHTTPConnectivity (line 1241), the code checks if host 'Contains ":"' to detect if a port is already present. But IPv6 addresses also contain colons (e.g., https://[::1]:8080/path). After stripping the scheme and path, an IPv6 address like '[::1]:8080' would match the Contains(':') check, setting port to '' and using the raw host as addr. The resulting dial would fail or connect to the wrong endpoint. Should use net.SplitHostPort or check for bracket-enclosed IPv6 first.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/slate","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:40Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:30:17Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T18:30:17Z","close_reason":"Closed"}

new_value: {"description":"wisps.go:1121-1141: addWispLabel and removeWispLabel perform INSERT/DELETE on wisp_labels but do NOT record events in wisp_events. In contrast, the permanent AddLabel/RemoveLabel (labels.go) DO record events via execContext INSERT INTO events. This means wisp label changes have no audit trail. Users running 'bd log' on a wisp won't see label changes. Suggested fix: add recordEventInTable calls for EventLabelAdded/EventLabelRemoved, matching labels.go behavior."}

old_value: {"id":"bd-zoi2","title":"federation PushTo/PullFrom/Fetch: missing SSH fallback for SSH remotes","description":"In federation.go, PushTo (line 16), PullFrom (30), and Fetch (51) always use CALL DOLT_PUSH/PULL/FETCH via SQL, with no SSH fallback. If a federation peer uses an SSH remote URL, these operations will time out the same way store.go's Push/Pull do without the SSH fallback. The store.go methods (Push, Pull, ForcePush) have SSH detection via isSSHRemote() and fall back to CLI (doltCLIPush/doltCLIPull), but federation.go does not replicate this pattern. Federation operations with SSH peers will silently hang or timeout.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:06:36Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:06:36Z"}

The store.go methods (Push, Pull, ForcePush) have SSH detection via isSSHRemote() and fall back to CLI (doltCLIPush/doltCLIPull), but federation.go does not replicate this pattern. Federation operations with SSH peers will silently hang or timeout."}

old_value: {"id":"bd-msdq","title":"Push/ForcePush/Pull: SSH fallback skipped when remoteUser is set for Hosted Dolt SSH remotes","description":"In store.go Push() (line 1299), ForcePush() (1335), and Pull() (1372), the SSH check (isSSHRemote) is only reached if remoteUser is empty. When remoteUser is set, the code goes directly to CALL DOLT_PUSH/PULL with --user flag via SQL. But if a Hosted Dolt remote also uses SSH transport (git+ssh://), the SQL procedure will still time out because the MySQL connection drops before SSH transfer completes — the exact problem the SSH fallback was designed to prevent. The SSH check should happen BEFORE the remoteUser check, or the remoteUser path should also use CLI for SSH URLs. This could cause silent push/pull failures for Hosted Dolt users with SSH remotes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:06:28Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:06:28Z"}

new_value: {"description":"In cmd/bd/dolt.go doltIdleMonitorCmd (line 363), the PID file is written immediately but only cleaned up in the SIGTERM/SIGINT handler (line 383). If the monitor exits normally (e.g., idle timeout disabled at line 371 with early return, or RunIdleMonitor returns normally at line 387), the PID file is left behind as an orphan. The early return at line 371 when idle timeout is disabled ('0') exits without cleaning up the PID file it just wrote on line 363. Should defer PID file cleanup."}

dependencies.go:68-87: AddDependency's cycle detection CTE only traverses the 'dependencies' table. Cross-table cycles involving wisp_dependencies are not detected. For example: permanent issue A depends on wisp B (in wisp_dependencies), wisp B depends on permanent issue A (in dependencies) — this cycle is not caught. Similarly, DetectCycles (line 694) only queries GetAllDependencyRecords which only reads the permanent dependencies table. Suggested fix: extend the cycle detection CTE to UNION both dependency tables, or run the check against both tables.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/ruby","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:42Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:31:32Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-x9oj","title":"doltCLIPush/doltCLIPull: no timeout on CLI exec, can hang indefinitely","description":"In store.go doltCLIPush (line 1259) and doltCLIPull (1276), exec.CommandContext is used with the caller's context. However, Push() and Pull() create their context from the incoming ctx which may have no deadline. If the SSH transfer hangs (network issue, DNS, SSH key prompt), the CLI process will block forever. The SQL path has MySQL driver timeouts, but the CLI path has none. Should add a timeout context (e.g., 5 minutes) wrapping the exec to prevent indefinite hangs. This is the exact scenario the CloseWithTimeout pattern in doltutil was designed for.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:06:45Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:06:45Z"}

{"description":"HIGH: mol_burn.go burnWisps() (lines 387-403) calls s.DeleteIssue() in a loop without a wrapping transaction. If a delete fails midway, you get a partially burned molecule. new_value: {"description":"credentials.go:47-53: encryptionKey() derives AES-256 key from SHA256(dbPath + hardcoded-salt). The dbPath is predictable (e.g., ~/.beads/.dolt), making the key guessable. Anyone with knowledge of the file path can decrypt stored federation passwords. The salt is a hardcoded string, not a random value. Additionally, moving the database to a different path silently breaks all encrypted credentials. Suggested fix: use a randomly-generated key stored securely (e.g., in a separate file with restrictive permissions, or system keyring), or at minimum add a per-installation random salt."}

new_value: {"description":"queries.go:16-34: SearchIssues calls searchWisps and if it returns an error, silently falls through to query the issues table instead. The error is completely discarded. This was added for backwards compatibility with pre-migration databases, but it masks real errors (query timeouts, connection failures, corrupt data). A timeout on the wisps query would result in silently returning stale/wrong results from the issues table. Suggested fix: distinguish between 'table does not exist' errors (legitimate fallthrough) and real errors (should be returned). Same pattern at lines 29-34 for allEphemeral(filter.IDs)."}

new_value: {"description":"In cmd/bd/dolt.go doltRemoteAddCmd (lines 630-649), the remote is first added to the SQL server, then to CLI. If the SQL add succeeds but CLI fails, the code prints a warning but reports success ('Added remote ... (SQL + CLI)' on line 658). The user sees a success message with the '(SQL + CLI)' suffix even though CLI failed. The success message should accurately reflect which surfaces succeeded. Similarly in doltRemoteRemoveCmd (lines 807-838), if SQL removal succeeds but CLI removal fails, the success message says 'Removed remote ... (SQL + CLI)' which is misleading."}

In cmd/bd/dolt.go doltRemoteAddCmd (lines 630-649), the remote is first added to the SQL server, then to CLI. If the SQL add succeeds but CLI fails, the code prints a warning but reports success ('Added remote ... (SQL + CLI)' on line 658). The user sees a success message with the '(SQL + CLI)' suffix even though CLI failed. The success message should accurately reflect which surfaces succeeded. Similarly in doltRemoteRemoveCmd (lines 807-838), if SQL removal succeeds but CLI removal fails, the success message says 'Removed remote ... old_value: {"id":"bd-w59l","title":"SearchIssues ephemeral fallthrough silently swallows errors","description":"queries.go:16-34: SearchIssues calls searchWisps and if it returns an error, silently falls through to query the issues table instead. The error is completely discarded. This was added for backwards compatibility with pre-migration databases, but it masks real errors (query timeouts, connection failures, corrupt data). A timeout on the wisps query would result in silently returning stale/wrong results from the issues table. Suggested fix: distinguish between 'table does not exist' errors (legitimate fallthrough) and real errors (should be returned). Same pattern at lines 29-34 for allEphemeral(filter.IDs).","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/mica","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:09Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:32:28Z"}

{"description":"HIGH: create.go createInRig() (lines 834-993) has two critical issues:

1. Missing Dolt commit for labels (lines 974-979): Labels added during cross-rig creation
   are not committed to Dolt. The main create path (lines 743-748) explicitly calls
   store.Commit() for post-create metadata, but createInRig never commits. Labels sit
   in the Dolt working set and can be lost on push/sync/restart. Same class of bug as GH#2009.

2. Major feature divergence: createInRig is missing compared to the main create path:
   - --deps flag (dependency creation)
   - --parent flag (parent-child relationships, label inheritance)
   - --estimate, --validate flags
   - --waits-for / --waits-for-gate flags
   - --no-inherit-labels handling
   - Agent label auto-add logic
   - Hook execution (hookRunner.Run)
   - SetLastTouchedID tracking
   - discoveredFromParentID / SourceRepo inheritance
   - MolType/WispType validation (no IsValid() check)
   - Event flag validation (event metadata on non-event types)

   Users creating cross-rig issues with any of these flags silently have them ignored.

Fix: Consolidate into a single code path operating on targetStore, or add all missing
features to createInRig. Consolidation strongly preferred to prevent ongoing divergence."}

new_value: {"description":"events.go:61-78: GetAllEventsSince uses UNION ALL between events and wisp_events tables, both of which use independent auto-increment IDs. The result is ORDER BY id ASC, but both tables can have events with the same ID (e.g., both tables have row ID=5). This produces ambiguous ordering and can cause event-streaming consumers to miss events or process them out of order. Suggested fix: either add a table discriminator to the ORDER BY (e.g., ORDER BY table_source, id ASC), use UNION ALL with created_at ordering instead, or use a single shared sequence."}

config.go:266 defines showConfigYAMLOverrides() with a TODO(bd-20j) comment but an empty function body. This function is called from configListCmd (line 258) after listing DB config, intended to warn when config.yaml or env vars override database settings.

The empty function means 'bd config list' silently shows DB values that may not match the effective config, leading to debugging confusion.

Fix: Either implement the override detection logic (check config.yaml and env var values against DB config, warn on mismatches) or remove the function and its call until ready to implement. If the work is already tracked as bd-20j, verify that bead exists and is discoverable."}

{"description":"dependencies.go:68-87: AddDependency's cycle detection CTE only traverses the 'dependencies' table. Cross-table cycles involving wisp_dependencies are not detected. For example: permanent issue A depends on wisp B (in wisp_dependencies), wisp B depends on permanent issue A (in dependencies) — this cycle is not caught. Similarly, DetectCycles (line 694) only queries GetAllDependencyRecords which only reads the permanent dependencies table. Suggested fix: extend the cycle detection CTE to UNION both dependency tables, or run the check against both tables."}

old_value: {"id":"bd-zoi2","title":"federation PushTo/PullFrom/Fetch: missing SSH fallback for SSH remotes","description":"In federation.go, PushTo (line 16), PullFrom (30), and Fetch (51) always use CALL DOLT_PUSH/PULL/FETCH via SQL, with no SSH fallback. If a federation peer uses an SSH remote URL, these operations will time out the same way store.go's Push/Pull do without the SSH fallback. The store.go methods (Push, Pull, ForcePush) have SSH detection via isSSHRemote() and fall back to CLI (doltCLIPush/doltCLIPull), but federation.go does not replicate this pattern. Federation operations with SSH peers will silently hang or timeout.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:06:36Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:30:57Z"}

{"description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-d76tx
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:34:39Z
dispatched_by: mayor

In federation.go, PushTo (line 16), PullFrom (30), and Fetch (51) always use CALL DOLT_PUSH/PULL/FETCH via SQL, with no SSH fallback. If a federation peer uses an SSH remote URL, these operations will time out the same way store.go's Push/Pull do without the SSH fallback. The store.go methods (Push, Pull, ForcePush) have SSH detection via isSSHRemote() and fall back to CLI (doltCLIPush/doltCLIPull), but federation.go does not replicate this pattern. Federation operations with SSH peers will silently hang or timeout."}
