old_value: {"id":"bd-v6v8","title":"Transaction SearchIssues supports far fewer filters than store-level","description":"transaction.go:182-261: doltTransaction.SearchIssues supports only 5 filter fields (query text, ParentID, Status, SpecIDPrefix, SourceRepo). The store-level DoltStore.SearchIssues supports ~25+ filter fields (Labels, Assignee, Priority, IssueType, MolType, WispType, date ranges, Pinned, NoAssignee, etc.). This means the same IssueFilter struct produces different results inside vs outside a transaction, which is a subtle API contract violation. Callers using RunInTransaction with complex filters will silently get unfiltered results. Suggested fix: align the transaction-level filter support with the store-level, or at minimum return an error if unsupported filters are passed.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:27Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:27Z"}

{"description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-zn7me
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:35:07Z
dispatched_by: beads/crew/emma

migrate_dolt_nocgo.go rejects 'bd migrate --to-dolt' with 'Dolt backend requires CGO' error. However, migrate_shim.go already provides a working SQLite extraction path via the system sqlite3 CLI that works without CGO. The auto-migration path (migrate_auto_nocgo.go) correctly delegates to shimMigrateSQLiteToDolt(), but the explicit --to-dolt path does not.

This means users with nocgo builds who want to explicitly migrate via 'bd migrate --to-dolt' are blocked, even though the auto-migration works fine on the same binary.

Fix: In migrate_dolt_nocgo.go, delegate to a shim-based migration function instead of rejecting outright. Use the same sqlite3 CLI extraction + importToDolt path that the auto-migration uses."}

{"description":"ephemeral_routing.go:251-262: PromoteFromEphemeral copies events and comments from wisp tables to permanent tables using '_, _ = s.execContext(...)' — both result and error are discarded. Then line 265 calls deleteWisp which removes the wisp and all wisp_* auxiliary records. If the copy fails (e.g., connection error, constraint violation), the promotion completes successfully but events/comments are permanently lost. Suggested fix: at minimum log the error; ideally make event/comment copy a required step or warn the user if data was lost during promotion."}

new_value: {"description":"In store.go doltCLIPush (line 1259) and doltCLIPull (1276), exec.CommandContext is used with the caller's context. However, Push() and Pull() create their context from the incoming ctx which may have no deadline. If the SSH transfer hangs (network issue, DNS, SSH key prompt), the CLI process will block forever. The SQL path has MySQL driver timeouts, but the CLI path has none. Should add a timeout context (e.g., 5 minutes) wrapping the exec to prevent indefinite hangs. This is the exact scenario the CloseWithTimeout pattern in doltutil was designed for."}

new_value: {"description":"wisps.go:522-767 vs queries.go:16-~300: searchWisps() and SearchIssues() duplicate ~200 lines of filter-building logic for WHERE clauses. Both build identical filters (Status, ExcludeStatus, IssueType, Assignee, Priority, IDs, Labels, date ranges, etc.) but targeting different tables. Any new filter field must be manually added to both functions, creating a maintenance burden and risk of divergence. Multiple filters already differ subtly (e.g., SearchIssues handles filter.Assignee with OR logic for unassigned, searchWisps uses simple equality). Suggested fix: extract shared filter-building into a function like buildIssueFilterClauses(filter, tableName) that returns (whereClauses, args)."}

new_value: {"description":"In store.go Push() (line 1299), ForcePush() (1335), and Pull() (1372), the SSH check (isSSHRemote) is only reached if remoteUser is empty. When remoteUser is set, the code goes directly to CALL DOLT_PUSH/PULL with --user flag via SQL. But if a Hosted Dolt remote also uses SSH transport (git+ssh://), the SQL procedure will still time out because the MySQL connection drops before SSH transfer completes — the exact problem the SSH fallback was designed to prevent. The SSH check should happen BEFORE the remoteUser check, or the remoteUser path should also use CLI for SSH URLs. This could cause silent push/pull failures for Hosted Dolt users with SSH remotes."}

{"description":"rename.go:14-109: UpdateIssueID updates references in permanent tables (issues, dependencies, events, labels, comments, issue_snapshots, compaction_snapshots, child_counters) but does NOT update references in wisp tables (wisp_dependencies, wisp_events, wisp_labels, wisp_comments). If a wisp has a dependency pointing to a renamed issue (e.g., wisp_dependencies.depends_on_id = oldID), that reference becomes dangling after the rename. Suggested fix: add UPDATE statements for wisp_dependencies (both issue_id and depends_on_id columns), wisp_events, wisp_labels, and wisp_comments."}

{"id":"bd-zoi2","title":"federation PushTo/PullFrom/Fetch: missing SSH fallback for SSH remotes","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-d76tx
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:34:39Z
dispatched_by: mayor

In federation.go, PushTo (line 16), PullFrom (30), and Fetch (51) always use CALL DOLT_PUSH/PULL/FETCH via SQL, with no SSH fallback. If a federation peer uses an SSH remote URL, these operations will time out the same way store.go's Push/Pull do without the SSH fallback. The store.go methods (Push, Pull, ForcePush) have SSH detection via isSSHRemote() and fall back to CLI (doltCLIPush/doltCLIPull), but federation.go does not replicate this pattern. Federation operations with SSH peers will silently hang or timeout.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/mica","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:06:36Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:38:41Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T18:38:41Z","close_reason":"Merged to main"}

new_value: {"status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"bd-is9q","title":"Massive filter-building duplication between searchWisps and SearchIssues","description":"wisps.go:522-767 vs queries.go:16-~300: searchWisps() and SearchIssues() duplicate ~200 lines of filter-building logic for WHERE clauses. Both build identical filters (Status, ExcludeStatus, IssueType, Assignee, Priority, IDs, Labels, date ranges, etc.) but targeting different tables. Any new filter field must be manually added to both functions, creating a maintenance burden and risk of divergence. Multiple filters already differ subtly (e.g., SearchIssues handles filter.Assignee with OR logic for unassigned, searchWisps uses simple equality). Suggested fix: extract shared filter-building into a function like buildIssueFilterClauses(filter, tableName) that returns (whereClauses, args).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:21Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:39:35Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-6py7","title":"burnWisps deletes issues without transaction - partial deletion on failure","description":"HIGH: mol_burn.go burnWisps() (lines 387-403) calls s.DeleteIssue() in a loop without a wrapping transaction. If a delete fails midway, you get a partially burned molecule. The function silently continues past errors and always returns nil. By contrast, squashMolecule properly wraps in transact(). Fix: wrap loop in transact() or use DoltStore.DeleteIssues() batch API.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:33Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/topaz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:32:18Z"}

new_value: {"status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"bd-e1ev","title":"AdvanceToNextStep TOCTOU race condition in multi-agent environment","description":"HIGH: mol_current.go AdvanceToNextStep() (lines 514-577) reads molecule progress, identifies next ready step, then writes to it -- all without a transaction. In multi-agent Gas Town, two agents could simultaneously close different steps, both identify the same next ready step, and both try to claim it. Classic read-then-write TOCTOU race. Fix: wrap read-identify-update in transact() or add optimistic concurrency control.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:37Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/topaz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:29:27Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-gp5b","title":"Embedded hook templates loaded but content never used - binary bloat + version drift","description":"MEDIUM: hooks.go (lines 18-36) embeds template files from templates/hooks/ via hooksFS. But installHooksWithOptions() only uses the embedded map for key names (line 454). Content is always generated by generateHookSection(). Templates have hardcoded v0.56.1 version while code uses dynamic Version variable. Also: installHooks() wrapper (line 420) is dead code -- never called. Fix: remove embedded templates and use static list of hook names, or remove installHooks wrapper.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:55Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/topaz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:32:26Z"}

new_value: {"status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"bd-rv8g","title":"Encryption key derivation from dbPath is predictable","description":"credentials.go:47-53: encryptionKey() derives AES-256 key from SHA256(dbPath + hardcoded-salt). The dbPath is predictable (e.g., ~/.beads/.dolt), making the key guessable. Anyone with knowledge of the file path can decrypt stored federation passwords. The salt is a hardcoded string, not a random value. Additionally, moving the database to a different path silently breaks all encrypted credentials. Suggested fix: use a randomly-generated key stored securely (e.g., in a separate file with restrictive permissions, or system keyring), or at minimum add a per-installation random salt.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:01Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:32:22Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-hl48","title":"GetAllEventsSince UNION ALL has auto-increment ID collisions","description":"events.go:61-78: GetAllEventsSince uses UNION ALL between events and wisp_events tables, both of which use independent auto-increment IDs. The result is ORDER BY id ASC, but both tables can have events with the same ID (e.g., both tables have row ID=5). This produces ambiguous ordering and can cause event-streaming consumers to miss events or process them out of order. Suggested fix: either add a table discriminator to the ORDER BY (e.g., ORDER BY table_source, id ASC), use UNION ALL with created_at ordering instead, or use a single shared sequence.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:38Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:33:37Z"}

{"id":"bd-9k0c","title":"setFederationCredentials: race window between env set and SQL exec","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-0tkty
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:25:47Z
dispatched_by: mayor

In credentials.go setFederationCredentials (line 264), DOLT_REMOTE_USER and DOLT_REMOTE_PASSWORD are set as process-wide environment variables. The federationEnvMutex serializes federation operations, but it does NOT protect against non-federation code that might also read these env vars or against other goroutines that might exec dolt CLI commands. In store.go Push/Pull, the SQL CALL is made between Setenv and Unsetenv — if another goroutine runs a dolt CLI command (like doltCLIPush) during that window, it would inherit the federation credentials. The env-var approach is fundamentally fragile for concurrent use.","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/quartz","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:02Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:25:47Z"}

new_value: {"description":"HIGH: list.go watchIssues() (lines 168-171) uses fsnotify to watch for writes to issues.jsonl or .db files. With Dolt, data is in a server-side database, not files. The watcher never fires. Also hardcodes .beads as relative path (line 121) instead of using beads.FindBeadsDir(). Additional issues: signal handler leak (line 152, signal.Stop never called), race condition in debounce timer callback (line 176, AfterFunc runs in separate goroutine without synchronization). Fix: either disable watch mode for Dolt with clear error, or implement polling-based refresh."}

{"notes":"Fix: replaced SHA256(dbPath+salt) key derivation with random 32-byte key stored in .encryption_key file (0600 perms). Added migration to re-encrypt existing credentials. Key file location: parent dir of dbPath (the .beads dir)."}
old_value: {"id":"bd-rv8g","title":"Encryption key derivation from dbPath is predictable","description":"credentials.go:47-53: encryptionKey() derives AES-256 key from SHA256(dbPath + hardcoded-salt). The dbPath is predictable (e.g., ~/.beads/.dolt), making the key guessable. Anyone with knowledge of the file path can decrypt stored federation passwords. The salt is a hardcoded string, not a random value. Additionally, moving the database to a different path silently breaks all encrypted credentials. Suggested fix: use a randomly-generated key stored securely (e.g., in a separate file with restrictive permissions, or system keyring), or at minimum add a per-installation random salt.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:01Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:54:50Z"}

{"id":"bd-9k0c","title":"setFederationCredentials: race window between env set and SQL exec","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-0tkty
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:25:47Z
dispatched_by: mayor

In credentials.go setFederationCredentials (line 264), DOLT_REMOTE_USER and DOLT_REMOTE_PASSWORD are set as process-wide environment variables. The federationEnvMutex serializes federation operations, but it does NOT protect against non-federation code that might also read these env vars or against other goroutines that might exec dolt CLI commands. In store.go Push/Pull, the SQL CALL is made between Setenv and Unsetenv — if another goroutine runs a dolt CLI command (like doltCLIPush) during that window, it would inherit the federation credentials. The env-var approach is fundamentally fragile for concurrent use.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/quartz","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:02Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:57:40Z"}

queries.go:16-34: SearchIssues calls searchWisps and if it returns an error, silently falls through to query the issues table instead. The error is completely discarded. This was added for backwards compatibility with pre-migration databases, but it masks real errors (query timeouts, connection failures, corrupt data). A timeout on the wisps query would result in silently returning stale/wrong results from the issues table. Suggested fix: distinguish between 'table does not exist' errors (legitimate fallthrough) and real errors (should be returned). Same pattern at lines 29-34 for allEphemeral(filter.IDs)."}

{"id":"bd-zw81","title":"Stale JSONL/SQLite references across doctor subsystem","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-x0l3b
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:24:25Z
dispatched_by: beads/crew/emma

Multiple files contain stale references to JSONL and SQLite that should be updated post-Dolt migration:

1. git.go line ~576: FixMergeDriver sets merge driver name to 'Beads JSONL merge driver'
2. validation.go line ~351: CheckGitConflicts only scans *.jsonl files, ignoring other text files
3. installation.go lines ~93-163: CheckUntrackedBeadsFiles entire check looks for untracked .jsonl files
4. agent.go lines ~718-722: enrichUntrackedFiles references JSONL files
5. gitignore.go lines ~374-387: CheckRedirectTargetValid falls through to checking for beads.db (SQLite)
6. federation.go lines 54,146,263,356,447: return 'N/A (SQLite backend)' messages
7. legacy.go line ~220: CheckDatabaseConfig checks for .db files (SQLite-only)
8. maintenance.go line ~390: message references 'JSONL fallback'
9. role.go line ~31: comment says 'stored in SQLite'
10. legacy.go line ~360: countJSONLIssuesAndPrefix uses default scanner buffer (64KB) unlike migration_validation.go which uses 2MB

Suggested fix: Remove or update references. For checks that only apply to legacy backends, either remove them or clearly gate and document them as legacy-only.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:09Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:24:34Z"}

{"id":"bd-7lgi","title":"verifyServerTarget uses hardcoded root credentials, ignores config","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-w0p1t
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:23:19Z
dispatched_by: beads/crew/emma

migrate_safety.go:121 verifyServerTarget() opens a MySQL connection with hardcoded DSN 'root@tcp(%s)/' — no password, always user 'root'. However, the Dolt server may be configured with different credentials (via metadata.json or env vars). If the server requires authentication, the verification query will fail even when the actual migration would succeed with the correct credentials.

By contrast, verifyMigrationData() (line 205) correctly accepts user/password parameters from the caller.

Fix: Add user and password parameters to verifyServerTarget() and use them in the DSN. Update callers in runMigrationPhases() to pass the configured credentials.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:09:13Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/opal","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:23:26Z"}

{"id":"bd-yzlh","title":"dolt clean-databases: SQL injection via backtick-quoted database names from SHOW DATABASES","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-qwo6f
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:22:37Z
dispatched_by: beads/crew/emma

In cmd/bd/dolt.go doltCleanDatabasesCmd (line 525), database names from SHOW DATABASES are interpolated into a DROP DATABASE statement using backtick quoting: fmt.Sprintf('DROP DATABASE `%s`', name). If a database name contains a backtick character, this could break out of the quoted identifier. While database names from SHOW DATABASES are typically safe, Dolt allows creating databases with unusual names. The nolint:gosec comment acknowledges the G201 warning but the defense ('name from SHOW DATABASES') isn't sufficient — should escape backticks within the name (replace ` with ``) or use a parameterized approach.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:18Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:25:08Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T20:25:08Z","close_reason":"Completed with no code changes (already fixed or pushed directly to main)"}

{"id":"bd-31bo","title":"Hook uninstall does not reset core.hooksPath + misleading --chain flag","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-zmnf4
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:23:03Z
dispatched_by: mayor

MEDIUM: hooks.go has three related issues: (1) uninstallHooks() (lines 542-598) never resets core.hooksPath after uninstall. If hooks were installed with --beads or --shared, core.hooksPath still points to .beads/hooks/ after uninstall, silently breaking all hooks. (2) --chain flag (lines 317-319) produces misleading success message claiming existing hooks renamed to .old -- this is a lie since section markers handle it differently. (3) Hook help text (lines 287-294) claims pre-commit syncs database, post-merge syncs, etc. -- none of this actually happens. Fix: reset hooksPath on uninstall, remove misleading --chain message, update help text.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:50Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/topaz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:23:09Z"}

{"id":"bd-8ifp","title":"validateRef rejects valid Dolt branch names with dots or slashes","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-l1exc
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:28:43Z
dispatched_by: mayor

history.go:15: validRefPattern = ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ rejects branch names containing dots or slashes. Dolt and git branch names commonly contain dots (e.g., 'release/v2.0', 'feature/auth.flow'). This pattern is used by getIssueAsOf, Diff, and CommitExists. Callers using branch names with dots or slashes will get 'invalid ref format' errors. Suggested fix: expand the pattern to allow dots and forward slashes: ^[a-zA-Z0-9_./-]+$ or use a more permissive pattern that excludes only dangerous characters (quotes, semicolons, backticks).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:10Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:28:51Z"}

{"id":"bd-9k0c","title":"setFederationCredentials: race window between env set and SQL exec","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-0tkty
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:25:47Z
dispatched_by: mayor

In credentials.go setFederationCredentials (line 264), DOLT_REMOTE_USER and DOLT_REMOTE_PASSWORD are set as process-wide environment variables. The federationEnvMutex serializes federation operations, but it does NOT protect against non-federation code that might also read these env vars or against other goroutines that might exec dolt CLI commands. In store.go Push/Pull, the SQL CALL is made between Setenv and Unsetenv — if another goroutine runs a dolt CLI command (like doltCLIPush) during that window, it would inherit the federation credentials. The env-var approach is fundamentally fragile for concurrent use.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:02Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:25:54Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T20:25:54Z","close_reason":"Completed with no code changes (already fixed or pushed directly to main)"}

{"id":"bd-7lgi","title":"verifyServerTarget uses hardcoded root credentials, ignores config","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-w0p1t
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:23:19Z
dispatched_by: beads/crew/emma

migrate_safety.go:121 verifyServerTarget() opens a MySQL connection with hardcoded DSN 'root@tcp(%s)/' — no password, always user 'root'. However, the Dolt server may be configured with different credentials (via metadata.json or env vars). If the server requires authentication, the verification query will fail even when the actual migration would succeed with the correct credentials.

By contrast, verifyMigrationData() (line 205) correctly accepts user/password parameters from the caller.

Fix: Add user and password parameters to verifyServerTarget() and use them in the DSN. Update callers in runMigrationPhases() to pass the configured credentials.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:09:13Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/opal","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:25:29Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T20:25:29Z","close_reason":"Completed with no code changes (already fixed or pushed directly to main)"}

new_value: {"description":"In cmd/bd/dolt.go doltCleanDatabasesCmd (line 525), database names from SHOW DATABASES are interpolated into a DROP DATABASE statement using backtick quoting: fmt.Sprintf('DROP DATABASE `%s`', name). If a database name contains a backtick character, this could break out of the quoted identifier. While database names from SHOW DATABASES are typically safe, Dolt allows creating databases with unusual names. The nolint:gosec comment acknowledges the G201 warning but the defense ('name from SHOW DATABASES') isn't sufficient — should escape backticks within the name (replace ` with ``) or use a parameterized approach."}

new_value: {"description":"MEDIUM: hooks.go has three related issues: (1) uninstallHooks() (lines 542-598) never resets core.hooksPath after uninstall. If hooks were installed with --beads or --shared, core.hooksPath still points to .beads/hooks/ after uninstall, silently breaking all hooks. (2) --chain flag (lines 317-319) produces misleading success message claiming existing hooks renamed to .old -- this is a lie since section markers handle it differently. (3) Hook help text (lines 287-294) claims pre-commit syncs database, post-merge syncs, etc. -- none of this actually happens. Fix: reset hooksPath on uninstall, remove misleading --chain message, update help text."}

{"id":"bd-31bo","title":"Hook uninstall does not reset core.hooksPath + misleading --chain flag","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-zmnf4
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T18:23:03Z
dispatched_by: mayor

MEDIUM: hooks.go has three related issues: (1) uninstallHooks() (lines 542-598) never resets core.hooksPath after uninstall. If hooks were installed with --beads or --shared, core.hooksPath still points to .beads/hooks/ after uninstall, silently breaking all hooks. (2) --chain flag (lines 317-319) produces misleading success message claiming existing hooks renamed to .old -- this is a lie since section markers handle it differently. (3) Hook help text (lines 287-294) claims pre-commit syncs database, post-merge syncs, etc. -- none of this actually happens. Fix: reset hooksPath on uninstall, remove misleading --chain message, update help text.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:50Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/topaz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:25:24Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T20:25:24Z","close_reason":"Completed with no code changes (already fixed or pushed directly to main)"}

new_value: {"description":"history.go:15: validRefPattern = ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ rejects branch names containing dots or slashes. Dolt and git branch names commonly contain dots (e.g., 'release/v2.0', 'feature/auth.flow'). This pattern is used by getIssueAsOf, Diff, and CommitExists. Callers using branch names with dots or slashes will get 'invalid ref format' errors. Suggested fix: expand the pattern to allow dots and forward slashes: ^[a-zA-Z0-9_./-]+$ or use a more permissive pattern that excludes only dangerous characters (quotes, semicolons, backticks)."}

new_value: {"description":"MEDIUM: list.go has no validation for three user-facing flags: (1) --status (line 412): cast to types.Status without IsValid() check. Typos silently return zero results. (2) --sort (line 274): invalid value falls through to default case (no sorting) but disables SQL limit (line 399-401), fetching ALL issues for no benefit. (3) --type (line 433): cast to types.IssueType without validation. Compare with --mol-type and --wisp-type which both validate. Fix: add IsValid() checks for all three flags matching the validation pattern used for mol-type/wisp-type."}

new_value: {"description":"MEDIUM: close.go has two issues: (1) Line 100: store.GetIssue error is discarded. If DB connection fails, nil issue is passed to validators which may panic on nil dereference or mask the real error as a validation error. Fix: check error and report real DB errors. (2) autoCloseCompletedMolecule (line 206-207) assumes molecule parent lives in same store as the step. For cross-rig molecules, this may silently fail. Fix: verify molecule root exists in same store before auto-close attempt."}

new_value: {"description":"queries.go:16-34: SearchIssues calls searchWisps and if it returns an error, silently falls through to query the issues table instead. The error is completely discarded. This was added for backwards compatibility with pre-migration databases, but it masks real errors (query timeouts, connection failures, corrupt data). A timeout on the wisps query would result in silently returning stale/wrong results from the issues table. Suggested fix: distinguish between 'table does not exist' errors (legitimate fallthrough) and real errors (should be returned). Same pattern at lines 29-34 for allEphemeral(filter.IDs)."}

old_value: {"id":"bd-hn4q","title":"findParentMolecule N+1 queries walking parent-child chain","description":"MEDIUM: mol_current.go findParentMolecule() (lines 375-420) issues GetDependencyRecords() and GetIssue() for each level walking up the tree. O(depth) round-trips per call. findInProgressMolecules calls this for every in-progress issue, creating O(in_progress * depth) queries. Deep molecule hierarchies cause noticeable latency. Fix: add recursive CTE query or cache molecule root ID as denormalized data on each child.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:13:38Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/topaz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T21:25:04Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-ttno","title":"list.go: missing input validation for --status, --sort, --type flags","description":"MEDIUM: list.go has no validation for three user-facing flags: (1) --status (line 412): cast to types.Status without IsValid() check. Typos silently return zero results. (2) --sort (line 274): invalid value falls through to default case (no sorting) but disables SQL limit (line 399-401), fetching ALL issues for no benefit. (3) --type (line 433): cast to types.IssueType without validation. Compare with --mol-type and --wisp-type which both validate. Fix: add IsValid() checks for all three flags matching the validation pattern used for mol-type/wisp-type.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:13:13Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/topaz","updated_at":"2026-03-01T21:25:07Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-w59l","title":"SearchIssues ephemeral fallthrough silently swallows errors","description":"queries.go:16-34: SearchIssues calls searchWisps and if it returns an error, silently falls through to query the issues table instead. The error is completely discarded. This was added for backwards compatibility with pre-migration databases, but it masks real errors (query timeouts, connection failures, corrupt data). A timeout on the wisps query would result in silently returning stale/wrong results from the issues table. Suggested fix: distinguish between 'table does not exist' errors (legitimate fallthrough) and real errors (should be returned). Same pattern at lines 29-34 for allEphemeral(filter.IDs).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:09Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T21:26:34Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-myn7r
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T21:30:00Z
dispatched_by: mayor

queries.go:16-34: SearchIssues calls searchWisps and if it returns an error, silently falls through to query the issues table instead. The error is completely discarded. This was added for backwards compatibility with pre-migration databases, but it masks real errors (query timeouts, connection failures, corrupt data). A timeout on the wisps query would result in silently returning stale/wrong results from the issues table. Suggested fix: distinguish between 'table does not exist' errors (legitimate fallthrough) and real errors (should be returned). Same pattern at lines 29-34 for allEphemeral(filter.IDs)."}
old_value: {"id":"bd-w59l","title":"SearchIssues ephemeral fallthrough silently swallows errors","description":"queries.go:16-34: SearchIssues calls searchWisps and if it returns an error, silently falls through to query the issues table instead. The error is completely discarded. This was added for backwards compatibility with pre-migration databases, but it masks real errors (query timeouts, connection failures, corrupt data). A timeout on the wisps query would result in silently returning stale/wrong results from the issues table. Suggested fix: distinguish between 'table does not exist' errors (legitimate fallthrough) and real errors (should be returned). Same pattern at lines 29-34 for allEphemeral(filter.IDs).","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/jasper","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:09Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/obsidian","updated_at":"2026-03-01T21:30:01Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-gzu5","title":"checkDoltLocks masks connection errors as healthy state","description":"In migration_validation.go lines ~447-450, if openDoltConn fails (server down, misconfigured), checkDoltLocks returns (false, '') which callers interpret as 'no locks detected.' CheckMigrationCompletion at line ~239 uses this to set result.DoltLocked, so a connection failure incorrectly reports DoltLocked: false.\n\nSuggested fix: Return a third error value or at minimum set a flag indicating the check could not run.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:44Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:44Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-6gs3","title":"Inconsistent return shape from RunDoltHealthChecksWithLock on connection failure","description":"In dolt.go lines ~140-151, when openDoltConn fails, RunDoltHealthChecksWithLock returns only 2 checks (errCheck and lockCheck) instead of the 6 checks returned on the happy path. Any caller that indexes into the result slice by position, or displays checks in a fixed layout, will get inconsistent results or a panic.\n\nSuggested fix: Return placeholder checks for the missing dimensions (Schema, Issue Count, Status, Phantom Databases) so the return shape is always consistent.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:39Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:39Z"}

In dolt.go lines ~140-151, when openDoltConn fails, RunDoltHealthChecksWithLock returns only 2 checks (errCheck and lockCheck) instead of the 6 checks returned on the happy path. Any caller that indexes into the result slice by position, or displays checks in a fixed layout, will get inconsistent results or a panic.

Suggested fix: Return placeholder checks for the missing dimensions (Schema, Issue Count, Status, Phantom Databases) so the return shape is always consistent."}

{"id":"bd-4hn4","title":"ScanForArtifacts and checkDatabaseConfigValues silently swallow errors","description":"Two functions silently discard errors that should be surfaced:

1. artifacts.go line ~90: ScanForArtifacts discards the filepath.Walk error with '_ ='. If the walk fails partway through (permission error, filesystem error), a partial report is returned without any indication it was incomplete. Callers report 'No classic artifacts detected' when the scan actually failed.

2. config_values.go lines ~370-374: checkDatabaseConfigValues returns empty issues slice when dolt.NewFromConfigWithOptions fails. Database corruption or permission issues are masked as 'all config values valid'.

Suggested fix: Return or report errors instead of swallowing them.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:27Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:12:27Z"}

1. artifacts.go line ~90: ScanForArtifacts discards the filepath.Walk error with '_ ='. If the walk fails partway through (permission error, filesystem error), a partial report is returned without any indication it was incomplete. Callers report 'No classic artifacts detected' when the scan actually failed.

2. config_values.go lines ~370-374: checkDatabaseConfigValues returns empty issues slice when dolt.NewFromConfigWithOptions fails. Database corruption or permission issues are masked as 'all config values valid'.

Suggested fix: Return or report errors instead of swallowing them."}

{"description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-ddvuz
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T23:44:00Z
dispatched_by: mayor

In legacy.go, all 15+ DoctorCheck structs use raw string literals ('ok', 'warning') for the Status field instead of StatusOK, StatusWarning, StatusError constants from types.go. If these constants are ever changed, legacy.go checks will produce incorrect status values.

Also, types.go defines Status as a raw string with no type safety. Using a typed string (type Status string) would provide compile-time checking.

Suggested fix: Replace all string literals with constants. Consider adding a typed Status."}

{"description":"In migration_validation.go lines ~447-450, if openDoltConn fails (server down, misconfigured), checkDoltLocks returns (false, '') which callers interpret as 'no locks detected.' new_value: {"description":"In fix/database_config.go, findActualDBFile (lines ~54-93) only searches for files ending in .db (SQLite databases). For the Dolt backend (where the database is a dolt/ directory), it returns empty string and the fix does nothing. The function name and parent documentation don't clarify this SQLite-only scope, making it a confusing no-op for Dolt users who run 'bd doctor --fix'.\n\nSuggested fix: Either handle Dolt directories or gate behind a SQLite backend check with a clear message."}

{"description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-kxj4a
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-01T23:50:56Z
dispatched_by: mayor

In git.go line ~687, when provider.GetOpenIssues(ctx) fails, FindOrphanedIssues returns ([]OrphanIssue{}, nil) — no error. This silently hides database connectivity failures, authentication issues, or other real errors. The caller CheckOrphanedIssues would report 'no orphans' when the check actually could not run.

Also, CheckGitWorkingTree and CheckGitUpstream (lines ~282-293, 383-431) run multiple exec.Command('git', ...) calls without a context timeout. If the git process hangs, the doctor check blocks indefinitely.

Suggested fix: Return the error from GetOpenIssues. Use exec.CommandContext with a timeout for git commands."}

old_value: {"id":"bd-d96s","title":"detectHookMarkerState does not validate marker count or order","description":"In hooks_migration.go lines ~189-201, detectHookMarkerState uses strings.Contains to check for hookMarkerBeginTag and hookMarkerEndTag. If a hook file has been corrupted to contain two BEGIN tags and one END tag (or two full marker blocks), the function returns hookMarkerStateValid. It should verify exactly one BEGIN and one END exist, and that BEGIN precedes END, to correctly detect corruption.\n\nSuggested fix: Count occurrences of each tag and verify order.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:56Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:56Z"}

{"id":"bd-s20p","title":"fix/validation.go openDoltDB missing timeout, password support; diverges from openFixDB","description":"In fix/validation.go, openDoltDB (line ~289) builds a DSN without timeout or parseTime, and does not read BEADS_DOLT_PASSWORD. Meanwhile, the near-identical openFixDB in fix/remotes.go (line ~97-101) includes both timeout and password support. This means:

1. OrphanedDependencies and ChildParentDependencies fix operations can hang indefinitely if Dolt server is unresponsive (no timeout)
2. If a password is configured, openDoltDB will fail authentication

Suggested fix: Unify openDoltDB and openFixDB into a single function with timeout and password support.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:42Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:42Z"}

dispatched_by: mayor

In fix/validation.go, openDoltDB (line ~289) builds a DSN without timeout or parseTime, and does not read BEADS_DOLT_PASSWORD. Meanwhile, the near-identical openFixDB in fix/remotes.go (line ~97-101) includes both timeout and password support. This means:

1. OrphanedDependencies and ChildParentDependencies fix operations can hang indefinitely if Dolt server is unresponsive (no timeout)
2. If a password is configured, openDoltDB will fail authentication

Suggested fix: Unify openDoltDB and openFixDB into a single function with timeout and password support.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"beads/polecats/onyx","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:42Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:09:39Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T00:09:39Z","close_reason":"Closed"}

new_value: {"description":"In hooks_migration.go lines ~189-201, detectHookMarkerState uses strings.Contains to check for hookMarkerBeginTag and hookMarkerEndTag. If a hook file has been corrupted to contain two BEGIN tags and one END tag (or two full marker blocks), the function returns hookMarkerStateValid. It should verify exactly one BEGIN and one END exist, and that BEGIN precedes END, to correctly detect corruption.\n\nSuggested fix: Count occurrences of each tag and verify order."}

{"description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-nywa7
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-02T03:07:34Z
dispatched_by: mayor

In fix/database_integrity.go sqliteIntegrityRecovery (lines ~113-117), if getBdBinary() fails, the function returns the error but does NOT restore the backup. The database file has already been moved at line ~103. Compare with doltIntegrityRecovery (lines ~58-61) which correctly restores the backup on getBdBinary() failure.

This leaves users with no database file at the expected path if getBdBinary() fails during SQLite recovery.

Suggested fix: Restore backupDB back to dbPath before returning the error, matching the Dolt recovery pattern."}

{"id":"bd-uz1x","title":"Federation checks: hardcoded port 8080 and possible wrong PID file path","description":"In federation.go:

1. Line ~112: remotesAPIPort is hardcoded to 8080. If the user configured a different remotesapi port, the check reports the wrong port as inaccessible. The port should be read from config, similar to how the SQL server port is derived via doltserver.DefaultConfig.

2. Line ~71: CheckFederationRemotesAPI looks for PID file at filepath.Join(doltPath, 'dolt-server.pid') (inside the dolt data directory), but the PID file is typically at filepath.Join(beadsDir, 'dolt-server.pid') as seen in gitignore.go line 42. Wrong path means the check incorrectly reports server as not running.

3. No test coverage exists for any federation checks.

Suggested fix: Read port from config. Fix PID file path. Add tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:33Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:33Z"}

{"id":"bd-md2p","title":"Relative path usage makes doctor checks fragile when run from subdirectories","description":"Multiple check functions construct paths relative to cwd instead of accepting a path parameter or resolving the project root:

1. gitignore.go line ~91: CheckGitignore uses filepath.Join('.beads', '.gitignore')
2. gitignore.go: CheckRedirectNotTracked, CheckLastTouchedNotTracked, FixRedirectTracking, FixLastTouchedTracking all use relative paths
3. claude.go lines ~111-112, 175-176, 222-223: isBeadsPluginInstalled, isMCPServerInstalled, hasClaudeHooks use filepath.Join('.claude', 'settings.json')

If bd doctor is run from a subdirectory of the project, these checks will look in the wrong location. In contrast, functions like CheckConfigValues correctly take an explicit repoPath parameter.

Suggested fix: Accept a path parameter or resolve the project root internally, consistent with other checks.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:16Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:16Z"}

{"id":"bd-lars","title":"Nocgo stubs return StatusOK for skipped checks, misleading users","description":"In checks_nocgo.go, all nocgo stubs (lines 8-57) return StatusOK for checks that are actually skipped (CheckMergeArtifacts, CheckDuplicateIssues, CheckTestPollution, etc.). Returning 'ok' suggests the checks ran and found no problems. Users of nocgo builds will see an all-green report without realizing their installation has not been fully validated.

Similarly in migration_validation_nocgo.go, CheckMigrationReadiness returns StatusOK while result.Ready is false and result.Errors contains an error — contradictory.

Also, MigrationValidationResult struct diverges between cgo and nocgo: nocgo is missing ForeignPrefixCount and ForeignPrefixes fields, creating a latent compilation hazard.

Suggested fix: Return StatusWarning with 'Skipped: requires CGO' for nocgo stubs. Sync the struct fields.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:59Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:59Z"}

In checks_nocgo.go, all nocgo stubs (lines 8-57) return StatusOK for checks that are actually skipped (CheckMergeArtifacts, CheckDuplicateIssues, CheckTestPollution, etc.). Returning 'ok' suggests the checks ran and found no problems. Users of nocgo builds will see an all-green report without realizing their installation has not been fully validated.

Similarly in migration_validation_nocgo.go, CheckMigrationReadiness returns StatusOK while result.Ready is false and result.Errors contains an error — contradictory.

Also, MigrationValidationResult struct diverges between cgo and nocgo: nocgo is missing ForeignPrefixCount and ForeignPrefixes fields, creating a latent compilation hazard.

Suggested fix: Return StatusWarning with 'Skipped: requires CGO' for nocgo stubs. Sync the struct fields."}

old_value: {"id":"bd-hwmb","title":"RunPerformanceDiagnostics calls os.Exit() from library code","description":"In perf.go lines ~21 and 25, RunPerformanceDiagnostics calls os.Exit(1) directly. Calling os.Exit from a library function prevents callers from handling errors, running deferred cleanup, or testing the function. Also, the global cpuProfileFile variable (line 11) is written by startCPUProfile and read by stopCPUProfile with no synchronization.\n\nSuggested fix: Return errors instead of calling os.Exit. Use a mutex for cpuProfileFile or restructure to avoid the global.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:50Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:50Z"}

dispatched_by: mayor

In perf.go lines ~21 and 25, RunPerformanceDiagnostics calls os.Exit(1) directly. Calling os.Exit from a library function prevents callers from handling errors, running deferred cleanup, or testing the function. Also, the global cpuProfileFile variable (line 11) is written by startCPUProfile and read by stopCPUProfile with no synchronization.

Suggested fix: Return errors instead of calling os.Exit. Use a mutex for cpuProfileFile or restructure to avoid the global."}

{"description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-kk6jv
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-02T03:22:40Z
dispatched_by: mayor

In perf_dolt.go line ~108, runDoltServerDiagnostics constructs DSN as 'root:@tcp(...)' ignoring the configured user and BEADS_DOLT_PASSWORD environment variable. Meanwhile, openDoltDB in dolt.go correctly reads user/password from config and environment. If the server has a non-root user or requires a password, the perf diagnostics will fail or connect with wrong credentials.

Additionally, no context timeout is set on diagnostic queries (line ~122 uses bare context.Background()). Queries could hang indefinitely against a slow or deadlocked server. Other functions use context.WithTimeout consistently.

Suggested fix: Use the same credential resolution as openDoltDB. Add context.WithTimeout."}
{"assignee":"beads/polecats/onyx","status":"hooked"}
old_value:
{"id":"bd-7zej","title":"N+1 query patterns in deep.go and migration_validation.go","description":"Two functions use N+1 query patterns that fire individual queries in a loop:

1. deep.go checkMoleculeIntegrity (~lines 482-496): runs a separate db.QueryRow for each molecule found (up to 100), issuing 101 queries. Could be done in a single JOIN query.

2. migration_validation.go compareDoltWithJSONL (~lines 429-443): calls store.GetIssue for each JSONL ID individually. For large migrations with thousands of issues, this fires thousands of queries. A single SELECT followed by set-difference in Go would be far more efficient.

Suggested fix: Replace per-item queries with batch queries or JOINs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:41Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:41Z"}

CheckRepoFingerprint also has no test coverage.\n\nSuggested fix: Add length check before slicing, or use a safe truncation helper.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:36Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:36Z"}

{"description":"In checks_nocgo.go, all nocgo stubs (lines 8-57) return StatusOK for checks that are actually skipped (CheckMergeArtifacts, CheckDuplicateIssues, CheckTestPollution, etc.). Returning 'ok' suggests the checks ran and found no problems. Users of nocgo builds will see an all-green report without realizing their installation has not been fully validated.

Similarly in migration_validation_nocgo.go, CheckMigrationReadiness returns StatusOK while result.Ready is false and result.Errors contains an error — contradictory.

Also, MigrationValidationResult struct diverges between cgo and nocgo: nocgo is missing ForeignPrefixCount and ForeignPrefixes fields, creating a latent compilation hazard.

Suggested fix: Return StatusWarning with 'Skipped: requires CGO' for nocgo stubs. Sync the struct fields."}
old_value:
{"id":"bd-lars","title":"Nocgo stubs return StatusOK for skipped checks, misleading users","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-lku7u
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-02T03:22:22Z
dispatched_by: mayor

In checks_nocgo.go, all nocgo stubs (lines 8-57) return StatusOK for checks that are actually skipped (CheckMergeArtifacts, CheckDuplicateIssues, CheckTestPollution, etc.). Returning 'ok' suggests the checks ran and found no problems. Users of nocgo builds will see an all-green report without realizing their installation has not been fully validated.

Similarly in migration_validation_nocgo.go, CheckMigrationReadiness returns StatusOK while result.Ready is false and result.Errors contains an error — contradictory.

Also, MigrationValidationResult struct diverges between cgo and nocgo: nocgo is missing ForeignPrefixCount and ForeignPrefixes fields, creating a latent compilation hazard.

Suggested fix: Return StatusWarning with 'Skipped: requires CGO' for nocgo stubs. Sync the struct fields.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"beads/polecats/obsidian","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:10:59Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:24:28Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T03:24:28Z","close_reason":"Closed"}

{"description":"Multiple check functions construct paths relative to cwd instead of accepting a path parameter or resolving the project root:

1. gitignore.go line ~91: CheckGitignore uses filepath.Join('.beads', '.gitignore')
2. gitignore.go: CheckRedirectNotTracked, CheckLastTouchedNotTracked, FixRedirectTracking, FixLastTouchedTracking all use relative paths
3. claude.go lines ~111-112, 175-176, 222-223: isBeadsPluginInstalled, isMCPServerInstalled, hasClaudeHooks use filepath.Join('.claude', 'settings.json')

If bd doctor is run from a subdirectory of the project, these checks will look in the wrong location. In contrast, functions like CheckConfigValues correctly take an explicit repoPath parameter.

Suggested fix: Accept a path parameter or resolve the project root internally, consistent with other checks."}
old_value:
{"id":"bd-md2p","title":"Relative path usage makes doctor checks fragile when run from subdirectories","description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-jq3ji
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-02T03:08:11Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Multiple check functions construct paths relative to cwd instead of accepting a path parameter or resolving the project root:

1. gitignore.go line ~91: CheckGitignore uses filepath.Join('.beads', '.gitignore')
2. gitignore.go: CheckRedirectNotTracked, CheckLastTouchedNotTracked, FixRedirectTracking, FixLastTouchedTracking all use relative paths
3. claude.go lines ~111-112, 175-176, 222-223: isBeadsPluginInstalled, isMCPServerInstalled, hasClaudeHooks use filepath.Join('.claude', 'settings.json')

If bd doctor is run from a subdirectory of the project, these checks will look in the wrong location. In contrast, functions like CheckConfigValues correctly take an explicit repoPath parameter.

Suggested fix: Accept a path parameter or resolve the project root internally, consistent with other checks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"beads/polecats/opal","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:11:16Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/jasper","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:29:31Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T03:29:31Z","close_reason":"Completed with no code changes (already fixed or pushed directly to main)"}
